docs.title: About the Game
docs.text: Coral Island is a vibrant tropical farming simulation game developed by Stairway Games, released in October 2023 (version 1.0). Set on a gorgeous Indonesian-inspired archipelago, you inherit your grandmother's old farm and work alongside diverse villagers to restore the island's ecosystem and community.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Details 🎮 Genre Farming Sim / Life Sim 👥 Mode Single-player 🏭 Developer Stairway Games 📅 Release October 2023 (1.0) 🖥️ Platform PC, PS5, Xbox Series X/S

docs.title: 🌴 Farming & Ranching
docs.text: Coral Island's farming system is built around seasonal crops suited to a tropical climate. The soil starts depleted and must be cleared of trash and debris — reflecting the game's core theme of environmental restoration. You can raise animals including cows, chickens, goats, and sheep, each requiring outdoor pasture and daily care.

docs.title: Unique Mechanics
docs.text:
• Diving — Explore the ocean floor to clean trash, discover artifacts, and gather resources
  • Pollination — Bees and insect farms boost crop yields organically
  • Automation — Sprinklers and auto-feeders unlock as you progress through the town rank system

docs.title: 🤿 Diving & Ocean Restoration
docs.text: A standout feature of Coral Island is its underwater exploration. Using diving gear, you descend into the coral reefs surrounding the island to collect trash, salvage artifacts for the museum, and find rare gems. Cleaning the ocean raises your "Ocean Rank," which unlocks better diving equipment and reveals new areas.

docs.title: Ocean Rank Tiers
docs.text:
Rank Requirement Reward 🥉 Bronze C Clean 20 trash Basic fins upgrade 🥈 Silver B Clean 100 trash Oxygen tank upgrade 🥇 Gold A Donate 10 artifacts Underwater fast-travel

Tip: Prioritize upgrading your diving gear before your fishing rod. Diving provides significantly more early-game income and materials than fishing in Coral Island, and cleaning the ocean unlocks new areas packed with valuable forage.

docs.title: 🐄 Coral Island — 动物养殖完全指南
docs.text: Coral Island 的动物系统比 Stardew Valley 更复杂：品质分四级（普通→银→金→锇），好感度直接影响产品品质，且有独立的宠物机制。本指南从经济角度拆解每只动物的真实 ROI。

docs.title: 🐔 鸡舍动物（Coop Animals）
docs.text:
动物 购买价 成熟天数 日产出 基础售价 产品加工 加工后售价 🐔 鸡 800g 3 天 蛋 ×1 50g 蛋黄酱 150g 🦆 鸭 1,200g 4 天 鸭蛋 ×1 70g 鸭蛋黄酱 200g 🐰 兔 2,000g 6 天 兔毛/兔脚 80g（毛） 布料 240g（布） 🦚 孔雀 3,000g 5 天 孔雀羽毛 ×1 150g — — 🐣 鸵鸟 4,000g 7 天 鸵鸟蛋×1 200g 鸵鸟蛋酱 450g

Tip: 鸵鸟虽然是购买价最高的鸡舍动物，但每个蛋直接卖出 200g 或加工成酱值 450g，单格日利润远超任何鸡舍动物。前提是能养到成熟，7 天等待期值得投入。

docs.title: 🐄 畜棚动物（Barn Animals）
docs.text:
动物 购买价 成熟天数 日产出 基础售价 产品加工 加工后售价 🐄 奶牛 1,500g 3 天 牛奶 ×1 100g 奶酪(200g)/黄油(150g)/布丁(250g) 见下文 🐑 绵羊 2,000g 4 天 羊毛 ×1 120g 布料 240g 🐐 山羊 2,500g 3 天 山羊奶 ×1 140g 山羊奶酪 280g 🐖 猪 4,000g 5 天 松露 ×(1-3)/次 200g/个 松露油 400g/个 🦌 羊驼 5,000g 6 天 羊驼毛 ×1 250g 高品质布料 400g

Tip: 猪是动物中的利润之王。松露是地面觅食物品（猪拱出来的），每天可产出 1-3 个，每个加工成松露油价值 400g。3 个松露油 = 1,200g/天。但母猪需要自由放养（不能关在畜棚），冬季不产出。

docs.title: 好感度获取表
docs.text: 行为 好感度增量 上限 🫱 抚摸（每日一次） +15 1 次/天 🧀 喂食（自动/手动） +5/次 — 🌿 放牧（吃草） +10/天 — 🎶 收音机（鸡舍装饰） +5/天 需要购买收音机 250g ❄️ 暖炉（冬季） +10/天 需要购买暖炉 500g

docs.title: 品质升级公式
docs.text:
最终品质 = 基础品质 × (1 + 好感度系数 + 畜牧技能加成 + 饲料加成)

因子 加成 说明 基础品质 100% 刚买入或低好感 满好感 +50% 5❤️ 时 畜牧技能 Lv.5 +10% 解锁 "优质产品" 天赋 畜牧技能 Lv.10 +25% 解锁 "大师牧人" 天赋 高级饲料 +20% 购买自动喂食器后搭配优质饲料

docs.title: 🏗️ 鸡舍/畜棚升级与自动喂食器 ROI
docs.text:
建筑 升级费用 材料需求 最大容量 自动喂食器价格 解锁功能 🐔 基础鸡舍 2,000g 木材×50, 石头×30 4 只 不可装 基本养鸡 🐔 中级鸡舍 5,000g 硬木×30, 银锭×5 8 只 3,000g 鸭/兔入住 🐔 高级鸡舍 10,000g 金锭×8, 精炼石英×5 12 只 3,000g 孔雀/鸵鸟 🐮 基础畜棚 3,000g 木材×80, 石头×40 4 只 不可装 基本奶牛/绵羊 🐮 中级畜棚 6,000g 硬木×40, 银锭×8 8 只 4,000g 山羊/猪 🐮 高级畜棚 12,000g 金锭×10, 精炼石英×8 12 只 4,000g 羊驼

自动喂食器 ROI 计算：

设备 成本 节省时间/天 天数回本 长期价值 鸡舍自动喂食器 3,000g 3 分钟 无需回本（时间） 解放双手，不用每天手动放草料 畜棚自动喂食器 4,000g 4 分钟 同上 配合自动集蛋器实现全自动化

Tip: 自动集蛋器（2,000g，中级鸡舍解锁）比自动喂食器更优先购买——它每天自动收集所有蛋/毛，省去挨个点击的时间。自动喂食器 + 自动集蛋器 = 真正的被动收入。

docs.title: 🐾 宠物系统
docs.text: 宠物 获取方式 成本 好处 🐕 狗（柯基/德牧/金毛） 宠物店购买（周日） 1,000g 每天送 1 件礼物，可增加动物好感 🐈 猫（橘猫/黑猫/三花） 宠物店购买（周日） 1,000g 每天送 1 件礼物，可赶鸟 🐇 兔 宠物店购买 800g 无特殊功能，纯陪伴 🦎 蜥蜴 潜水发现后收养 免费 无特殊功能，纯收集 🐢 龟 海滩事件触发 免费 后院放养，吃杂草

docs.title: 宠物好感度
docs.text: 等级 效果 1-3❤️ 每天送 1 件随机小礼物（种子/觅食/贝壳） 4❤️ 解锁宠物抚摸加成：抚摸宠物后+1 体力再生（全天） 5❤️ 解锁宠物技能：狗帮忙赶走袭击动物的野生动物，猫帮忙抓老鼠保护饲料

docs.title: 奶制品利润表
docs.text: 产品 原料 加工机器 加工时间 售价 利润增幅 🥛 牛奶（基础） — — — 100g — 🧀 奶酪 牛奶×1 奶酪机 6 小时 200g +100g (+100%) 🧈 黄油 牛奶×1 黄油机 4 小时 150g +50g (+50%) 🍮 布丁 牛奶×1 布丁机 8 小时 250g +150g (+150%) 🥛 山羊奶 — — — 140g — 🧀 山羊奶酪 山羊奶×1 奶酪机 6 小时 280g +140g (+100%) 🥚 鸵鸟蛋 — — — 200g — 🫙 鸵鸟蛋酱 鸵鸟蛋×1 蛋黄酱机 4 小时 450g +250g (+125%)

docs.title: 蛋类加工利润表
docs.text:
产品 原料 加工机器 加工时间 售价 利润增幅 🥚 鸡蛋 — — — 50g — 🫙 普通蛋黄酱 鸡蛋×1 蛋黄酱机 3 小时 150g +100g (+200%) 🥚 鸭蛋 — — — 70g — 🫙 鸭蛋黄酱 鸭蛋×1 蛋黄酱机 3 小时 200g +130g (+186%) 🧶 羊毛/兔毛 — — — 80~120g — 🧥 布料 毛料×1 织布机 4 小时 240g +120~160g

Tip: 加工优先级：布丁 > 蛋黄酱 > 奶酪 > 黄油。布丁的利润增幅（+150%）最高但时间最长，适合睡觉前放入机器。如果机器数量有限，蛋黄酱的 +200% 增幅时间比最短，是最高效的选择。

docs.title: 💰 每日利润对比：动物 vs 作物
docs.text: 项目 单格日利润（基础） 加工后 加工后日利润/格 稳定性 🌱 春季作物平均 8~15g/格 加工翻倍 16~30g/格 ❌依赖季节 🐔 鸡（1 只） 50g 蛋黄酱 150g 150g/天 ✅全年 🦆 鸭（1 只） 70g 鸭蛋黄酱 200g 200g/天 ✅全年 🐄 奶牛（1 只） 100g 布丁 250g 250g/天 ✅全年 🐖 猪（1 只） 400~600g 松露油 800~1,200g 800~1,200g/天 ❌冬季无产出 🐰 兔（1 只） 80g 布料 240g 240g/天 ✅全年

docs.title: 结论
docs.text:
• 猪的单格利润最高（800-1,200g/天）但冬季停产，适合作为夏季秋季主力收入
  • 奶牛+布丁是最稳定的被动收入，全年无休
  • 动物整体利润远超作物（不含加工），但前期投入也大。推荐混合策略：春季种田积累资金 → 夏季扩建畜棚买牛/猪 → 秋季依靠畜产品度过冬季

docs.title: ❄️ 四季养殖策略
docs.text:
季节 策略重点 注意事项 🌸 春 买鸡起步（成本低、回报快）→ 攒钱升级鸡舍到中级 → 购入第一只鸭 春季作物是主要收入，动物是副线 ☀️ 夏 畜棚升级 → 买第一只奶牛 → 买猪（松露夏季价值最高） 猪需要自由放牧，留出草地空间 🍂 秋 进阶畜棚 → 买鸵鸟/羊驼 → 大量加工畜产品卖秋收高价 囤积松露油和布丁，为冬季储备 ❄️ 冬 猪停产，靠奶牛/鸡/羊驼维持收入 → 升级自动喂食器和集蛋器 冬季放牧无效，需要囤积干草

Tip: 冬季所有动物必须关在室内靠干草喂养。建议秋季至少囤积 100 捆干草（每个动物每天消耗 1 捆）从 Macy 的杂货店买干草 10g/捆，或在秋季用镰刀收割野草自制。每个动物一冬季（28 天）消耗 28 捆干草，6 只动物 = 168 捆 × 10g = 1,680g，值得提前准备。

docs.title: 🏭 Coral Island — 工匠设备与加工利润完全指南
docs.text: 加工是 Coral Island 财富增长的核心引擎——一个价值 50g 的鸡蛋经过蛋黄酱机变成 150g（+200%），一个 260g 的甜瓜经过压榨机变成 740g 甜瓜酒（+185%）。本指南拆解所有机器的经济模型，帮你做出最优加工决策。

docs.title: ⚙️ 全部工匠设备总览
docs.text: 机器 解锁条件 制作材料 加工时间 输入 输出 🫙 糖化罐 (Preserve Jar) 耕种 Lv.3 木材×30 + 石头×15 + 铜锭×3 12 小时 蔬菜/水果 腌菜/果酱 🍷 压榨机 (Keg) 耕种 Lv.6 木材×40 + 铜锭×5 + 银锭×2 24 小时（果）/ 48 小时（蔬菜） 水果/蔬菜/谷物 酒/果汁/醋/清酒 🧀 奶酪机 (Cheese Press) 畜牧 Lv.2 铜锭×3 + 石头×20 6 小时 牛奶/山羊奶 奶酪/山羊奶酪 🧈 黄油机 (Butter Churn) 畜牧 Lv.3 银锭×2 + 硬木×15 4 小时 牛奶 黄油 🍮 布丁机 (Pudding Maker) 畜牧 Lv.5 金锭×3 + 精炼石英×2 8 小时 牛奶 布丁 🫙 蛋黄酱机 (Mayo Machine) 畜牧 Lv.1 铜锭×2 + 木材×15 3 小时 蛋类 蛋黄酱 🧥 织布机 (Loom) 畜牧 Lv.4 银锭×3 + 硬木×20 4 小时 羊毛/兔毛 布料 🥩 烘干机 (Dehydrator) 耕种 Lv.8 金锭×3 + 精炼石英×3 8 小时 辣椒/羽衣甘蓝/西蓝花 干制蔬菜 🫒 榨油机 (Oil Press) 耕种 Lv.5 银锭×2 + 石材×15 6 小时 松露/向日葵/玉米 松露油/食用油 🐝 蜂箱 (Bee House) 中期任务 木材×20 + 铁锭×2 + 蜂后 72 小时（花附近） 花+花蜜 蜂蜜/特种蜜 🎣 发酵桶 (Fermenter) 耕种 Lv.10 金锭×5 + 锇锭×3 36~48 小时 酒 陈酿酒

docs.title: 糖化罐（Preserve Jar）— 12 小时
docs.text: 输入作物 基础价 糖化后价 利润增幅 每小时利润 🍉 西瓜 (Watermelon) 300g 700g +400g (+133%) 33.3g/h 🎃 南瓜 (Pumpkin) 280g 660g +380g (+136%) 31.7g/h 🍈 甜瓜 (Melon) 260g 620g +360g (+138%) 30.0g/h 🥦 花椰菜 (Cauliflower) 250g 590g +340g (+136%) 28.3g/h 🥦 西蓝花 (Broccoli) 185g 470g +285g (+154%) 23.8g/h 🥬 卷心菜 (Cabbage) 120g 320g +200g (+167%) 16.7g/h 🍅 番茄 (Tomato) 110g 280g +170g (+155%) 14.2g/h 🫐 蓝莓 (Blueberry) 140g 340g +200g (+143%) 16.7g/h 🥒 茄子 (Eggplant) 120g 310g +190g (+158%) 15.8g/h

docs.text: 输入作物 基础价 酒/果汁价 时间 利润增幅 每小时利润 🍈 甜瓜 (Melon) 260g 740g (甜瓜酒) 24h +480g (+185%) 20.0g/h 🎃 南瓜 (Pumpkin) 280g 690g (南瓜酒) 24h +410g (+146%) 17.1g/h 🍉 西瓜 (Watermelon) 300g 700g (西瓜酒) 24h +400g (+133%) 16.7g/h 🥦 花椰菜 250g 620g 24h +370g (+148%) 15.4g/h 🍇 葡萄 (Grape) 85g 340g (葡萄酒) 24h +255g (+300%) 10.6g/h 🌾 水稻 (Rice) 130g 390g (清酒) 24h +260g (+200%) 10.8g/h ☕ 咖啡豆 (Coffee Bean) 65g 250g (咖啡) 24h +185g (+285%) 7.7g/h 🥕 胡萝卜 (Carrot) 68g 200g (胡萝卜汁) 24h +132g (+194%) 5.5g/h 🌽 玉米 (Corn) 120g 310g (玉米汁) 48h +190g (+158%) 4.0g/h

docs.title: 烘干机（Dehydrator）— 8 小时
docs.text:
输入作物 基础价 干制售价 利润增幅 每小时利润 🌶️ 辣椒 (Hot Pepper) 85g 230g +145g (+171%) 18.1g/h 🥬 羽衣甘蓝 (Kale) 100g 270g +170g (+170%) 21.3g/h 🥦 西蓝花 (Broccoli) 185g 470g +285g (+154%) 35.6g/h

Tip: 西蓝花烘干是全局每小时利润最高的加工路线——35.6g/h 超过糖化罐的 33.3g/h。但烘干机只能处理这三种作物，适用范围有限。秋季西蓝花/羽衣甘蓝丰收时优先走烘干路线。

docs.title: 🔄 每种作物最佳加工路线选择
docs.text: 作物 季节 糖化罐 压榨机 烘干机 加工 最佳选择 🍉 西瓜 ☀️夏 700g ✓ 700g ✓ — — 糖化罐（12h vs 24h，同价更快） 🍈 甜瓜 ☀️夏 620g 740g ✓ — — 压榨机（溢价最高） 🎃 南瓜 🍂秋 660g 690g ✓ — — 压榨机糖化罐皆可（差价 30g，看你机器数量） 🥦 花椰菜 🌸春 590g ✓ 620g — — 压榨机（高 30g，等 24h 值得） 🥦 西蓝花 🌸春 470g — 470g ✓ — 糖化罐或烘干机均可（同价，烘干机更快） 🌶️ 辣椒 ☀️夏 — — 230g ✓ — 烘干机（唯一路线） 🍅 番茄 ☀️夏 280g ✓ — — — 糖化罐 🫐 蓝莓 ☀️夏 340g ✓ — — — 糖化罐 🍇 葡萄 🍂秋 — 340g ✓ — — 压榨机→葡萄酒 🌾 水稻 🍂秋 — 390g ✓ — — 压榨机→清酒 ☕ 咖啡豆 ☀️夏 — 250g ✓ — — 压榨机→咖啡 🍓 草莓 🌸春 360g — — — 糖化罐（草莓不能进压榨机）

docs.title: 你需要多少台机器
docs.text: 机器类型 前期（50 格田） 中期（150 格田） 后期（300+ 格田） 🫙 糖化罐 3~4 台 8~12 台 20~30 台 🍷 压榨机 2~3 台 6~10 台 15~25 台 🧀 奶酪机 1 台 2 台 4~6 台 🫙 蛋黄酱机 1~2 台 3~5 台 8~12 台 🥩 烘干机 1~2 台 3~4 台 6~8 台

docs.title: 布局原则
docs.text:
1. 分组摆放：5 台机器为一组，留 1 格走道
  2. 靠近仓库：机器贴着仓库放置，减少跑路时间
  3. 糖化罐＞压榨机优先：前期机器数量有限时，优先造糖化罐（12h vs 24h，更快的资金周转）
  4. 畜产品机器放畜棚内：奶酪机/黄油机/蛋黄酱机放在畜棚里，挤奶→加工一步到位
  5. 户外 vs 室内：压榨机和糖化罐可以放室外，建议做棚顶避雨（虽然游戏没有损坏机制，但整齐）

Tip: 当你有 10 台糖化罐和 5 台压榨机后，不要继续增建普通机器。转而投资发酵桶（Fermenter）——将压榨机产的普通酒陈酿升级为陈酿酒，售价再翻 1.5~2 倍。一个甜瓜酒（740g）陈酿后约值 1,200g。

docs.title: 🌟 工匠技能树（Artisan Skill Tree）
docs.text: 技能等级 技能名称 效果 是否必点 Lv.1 🥘 基础烹饪 解锁糖化罐 ✅ Lv.3 🫙 保鲜术 糖化罐产出 +10% 售价 ✅ Lv.5 🧈 品质提升 加工品有 15% 概率双倍产出 ✅✅核心 Lv.7 ⚡ 效率专家 所有机器加工时间 -15% ✅✅核心 Lv.9 💰 溢价谈判 加工品售价 +20% ✅✅核心 Lv.10 🏆 超级工匠 加工品可达到锇品质 ✅（终局）

docs.title: 加点顺序建议
docs.text:
前期优先： Lv.1 → Lv.3（糖化罐 +10% 价）→ Lv.5（双倍产出）

中期转压榨机流： Lv.7（加速）→ Lv.9（+20%售价）

终局： Lv.10 解锁锇品质加工品，单件售价再翻 1.5 倍

docs.title: 理想配比（300+ 格农场）
docs.text: 配置项 数量 单周期产出 说明 🍷 压榨机 (Keg) 20 台 20 瓶酒/天 主力加工设备 🫙 糖化罐 (Preserve Jar) 15 台 30 罐/天 快速周转补充 🥩 烘干机 (Dehydrator) 8 台 24 份/天 西蓝花专用 🧀 奶酪机/布丁机 6 台 12 个/天 畜产品处理 🫙 蛋黄酱机 8 台 24 瓶/天 鸡蛋加工 🎣 发酵桶 (Fermenter) 6 桶 3~6 桶陈酿酒/天 最高端路线

docs.title: 每日估算收入
docs.text:
时段 日收入估算 收入来源 🌸 春季 15,000~25,000g 花椰菜酒 + 西蓝花干 + 蛋黄酱 ☀️ 夏季 25,000~40,000g 甜瓜酒 + 西瓜罐 + 番茄罐 + 松露油 🍂 秋季 30,000~50,000g 南瓜酒 + 葡萄酒 + 陈酿发酵 ❄️ 冬季 5,000~10,000g 畜产品 + 陈酿酒库存出清

Tip: 冬季前必须陈酿一批甜瓜酒/南瓜酒。发酵桶里的酒会在冬季完成陈酿，冬季正是清仓卖高价的最佳时机——此时农场没有新鲜作物需要加工，机器全部闲置，陈酿酒就是唯一的加工收入。

docs.title: 📊 生卖 vs 加工全对比速查
docs.text:
输入 生卖价 最佳加工品 加工价 倍数 机器 时间 🥚 鸡蛋 50g 蛋黄酱 150g ×3.0 蛋黄酱机 3h 🥛 牛奶 100g 布丁 250g ×2.5 布丁机 8h 🍇 葡萄 85g 红酒 340g ×4.0 压榨机 24h ☕ 咖啡豆 65g 咖啡 250g ×3.8 压榨机 24h 🥦 西蓝花 185g 干西蓝花/罐 470g ×2.5 烘干机/罐 8h 🍈 甜瓜 260g 甜瓜酒 740g ×2.8 压榨机 24h 🐖 松露 200g 松露油 400g ×2.0 榨油机 6h 👕 羊毛 120g 布料 240g ×2.0 织布机 4h

黄金法则： 任何可加工的物品，永远不要生卖。哪怕只有一个蛋黄酱机，也把鸡蛋加工了再卖。3 小时换 +100g 的 ROI，没有任何活动比得上。

docs.title: Coral Island — Crop Profit Guide
docs.text:
Master your farm's economy in Coral Island with this complete seasonal crop profit guide. Each table includes seed price, growth time, sell price, and profit per day (base quality).

Note: Prices are base quality (regular). Fertilizer, farming skill levels, and crop quality (silver/gold/osmium) increase sell prices.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
• Early Spring: Plant Carrots and Radishes — fast turnaround for early cash flow.
  • Summer staple: Tomatoes are excellent early-regrowth crops for consistent income.
  • Fall jackpot: Pumpkins are the #1 profit crop in the game — plant as many as you can.
  • Winter farming: Crystal Cauliflower matches summer melons for profit/day.
  • Processing priority: Put Melon, Pumpkin, and Watermelon through Kegs for massive profit boosts.
  • Regrowth crops: Strawberry, Cranberry, and Frost Berry save replanting costs over long seasons.

Data source: Coral Island Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Coral Island Crops Database
docs.text: Welcome to the ultimate Coral Island crops database. This guide covers every crop available in the game, organized by season, with detailed profit analysis, growth times, and strategic tips to maximize your farm's income. Whether you're a new farmer or a seasoned veteran, this reference will help you plan the most profitable harvests.

docs.title: How Crop Profit is Calculated
docs.text:
In Coral Island, each crop has a base sell price, but your actual profit depends on several factors: - Seed Cost: The price to buy seeds from Sam's General Store or other vendors. - Growth Time: Days from planting to first harvest. - Regrowth: Some crops produce multiple harvests (e.g., Blueberries, Corn). - Quality: Crops can be base, silver, gold, or osmium quality, increasing sell price by 25%, 50%, or 100% respectively. - Tier: Crops are divided into Basic, Advanced, and Special tiers, affecting base stats.

Profit per day (PPD) is calculated as: (Sell Price - Seed Cost) / Growth Days. For regrowing crops, we use the average over the season.

docs.title: Spring Crops
docs.text: Spring is the starting season for most players. Focus on high-profit crops to build your capital.

docs.title: Spring Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Potato 50 6 No 80 5.0 Good early-game profit Cauliflower 80 12 No 175 7.9 High single-harvest value Strawberry 100 8 Yes (4 days) 120 10.0 Best spring regrower Green Bean 60 10 Yes (3 days) 90 6.0 Consistent income Rhubarb 100 13 No 220 9.2 High profit, long wait Garlic 40 4 No 60 5.0 Fastest spring crop Kale 70 6 No 110 6.7 Decent early option Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Excellent regrower

docs.title: Spring Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Strawberry (PPD: 10.0) – The undisputed king of spring. Plant on Day 1 for maximum harvests.
  2. Rhubarb (PPD: 9.2) – High single-harvest value, but only one harvest per season.
  3. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Great for steady income with regrowth.
  4. Cauliflower (PPD: 7.9) – Solid choice if you need a large lump sum.
  5. Kale (PPD: 6.7) – Good for early cash flow.

Tip: Plant Strawberries as soon as possible. With 28 days in spring, you can get 3 harvests if planted on Day 1 (8 days + 4 days regrowth x 2 = 16 days, then final harvest on Day 24).

docs.title: Summer Crops
docs.text: Summer offers the highest profit potential in Coral Island. Prioritize crops with regrowth for maximum returns.

docs.title: Summer Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Melon 120 12 No 250 10.8 Best single-harvest summer crop Tomato 60 6 Yes (3 days) 80 10.0 Excellent regrower Blueberry 80 7 Yes (4 days) 100 8.6 Carries over from spring Hot Pepper 40 5 Yes (3 days) 60 8.0 Fast regrowth cycle Corn 100 14 Yes (4 days) 150 7.1 Long growth, but multiple harvests Sunflower 80 8 No 180 12.5 Highest PPD in summer Radish 50 6 No 90 6.7 Quick filler crop Wheat 30 4 No 50 5.0 Low profit, used for crafting

docs.title: Summer Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Sunflower (PPD: 12.5) – Surprising top performer. Single harvest, but incredible profit.
  2. Melon (PPD: 10.8) – Classic summer cash crop.
  3. Tomato (PPD: 10.0) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  4. Hot Pepper (PPD: 8.0) – Fast regrowth, great for early summer.
  5. Blueberry (PPD: 8.6) – Still excellent if planted in spring.

Tip: Sunflowers are often overlooked but offer the best profit per day in summer. Plant them in bulk for a massive payout on Day 8.

docs.title: Fall Crops
docs.text: Fall introduces high-value crops that can be processed into artisan goods for even greater profit.

docs.title: Fall Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Pumpkin 150 13 No 320 13.1 Highest PPD in fall Cranberry 100 7 Yes (5 days) 130 10.0 Best regrower Eggplant 80 7 Yes (5 days) 100 8.6 Solid regrower Yam 60 10 No 160 10.0 High single-harvest value Bok Choy 50 4 No 80 7.5 Fastest fall crop Artichoke 120 8 No 220 12.5 Excellent profit Grape 60 10 Yes (3 days) 80 6.0 Good for wine Rice 40 8 No 100 7.5 Used for cooking

docs.title: Fall Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Pumpkin (PPD: 13.1) – The absolute best profit per day in fall. Plant on Day 1 for a Day 14 harvest.
  2. Artichoke (PPD: 12.5) – Close second, no regrowth but high value.
  3. Cranberry (PPD: 10.0) – Excellent regrower for consistent income.
  4. Yam (PPD: 10.0) – Great single-harvest option.
  5. Eggplant (PPD: 8.6) – Reliable regrower.

Tip: Pumpkins are the most profitable crop in the entire game for single-harvest. Use fertilizer to increase quality for even higher returns.

docs.title: Winter Crops
docs.text: Winter is unique in Coral Island. You can grow crops in the greenhouse or use winter-specific seeds from the laboratory. Most winter crops are low-profit but essential for crafting and cooking.

docs.title: Winter Crop Table
docs.text: Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Snowdrop 30 5 No 60 6.0 Used for winter seeds Frost Berry 50 7 Yes (4 days) 80 7.1 Best winter regrower Ice Root 60 10 No 130 7.0 High single-harvest Winter Wheat 20 4 No 40 5.0 Quick filler Crystal Fruit 80 12 No 200 10.0 Rare, high profit

docs.title: Winter Profit Rankings
docs.text:
1. Crystal Fruit (PPD: 10.0) – Best winter crop, but seeds are hard to find.
  2. Frost Berry (PPD: 7.1) – Reliable regrower for steady income.
  3. Ice Root (PPD: 7.0) – Good single-harvest option.
  4. Snowdrop (PPD: 6.0) – Decent for quick cash.
  5. Winter Wheat (PPD: 5.0) – Only for crafting.

Tip: Focus on the greenhouse during winter. Plant high-value summer or fall crops inside to maintain income. Crystal Fruit seeds can be obtained from the Winter Mystery Seed quest.

docs.title: Year-Round Crops (Greenhouse)
docs.text:
These crops can be grown any time in the greenhouse or with specific upgrades.

Crop Seed Cost Growth Days Regrowth Base Sell Price Profit per Day (PPD) Notes Coffee Bean 100 10 Yes (2 days) 150 25.0 Best PPD in game Tea Leaf 80 8 Yes (3 days) 120 20.0 Excellent for tea Ancient Fruit 200 28 Yes (7 days) 550 12.5 Rare, high value

Tip: Coffee Beans are the most profitable crop in Coral Island when grown in the greenhouse. They regrow every 2 days, giving you massive income.

docs.title: Artisan Goods Processing
docs.text:
Processing crops into artisan goods significantly increases profit. Here are the best crops to process:

Crop Base Sell Price Processed Good Processed Price Profit Increase Pumpkin 320 Pumpkin Juice 640 100% Melon 250 Melon Wine 500 100% Grape 80 Grape Wine 320 300% Coffee Bean 150 Coffee 300 100% Strawberry 120 Strawberry Jam 360 200%

Tip: Grapes have the highest profit multiplier when turned into wine. Use kegs to process them for maximum returns.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Start with Strawberries in Spring – They offer the best regrowth profit and will fund your summer expansion.
  2. Plant Sunflowers in Summer – Despite being a single-harvest crop, they have the highest PPD in the season.
  3. Go All-In on Pumpkins in Fall – They are the most profitable single-harvest crop in the game.
  4. Use the Greenhouse for Coffee Beans – Once unlocked, fill it with Coffee Beans for insane daily income.
  5. Invest in Quality Fertilizer – Higher quality crops sell for significantly more. Use it on your highest-value crops.
  6. Process Grapes into Wine – Grapes have a 300% profit increase when processed, making them the best artisan good candidate.
  7. Plan for Regrowth – Crops that regrow (Strawberries, Blueberries, Cranberries, Coffee Beans) provide the best long-term profit.
  8. Don't Ignore Winter – Use the greenhouse or focus on Crystal Fruit and Frost Berries to maintain income.
  9. Upgrade Your Watering Can – More crops mean more watering. Upgrade early to save time and energy.
  10. Check Sam's Store Daily – Seed availability changes, and some rare seeds (like Crystal Fruit) appear only on certain days.

Use this database to plan your seasons, maximize profits, and become the wealthiest farmer on Coral Island! For more guides, visit Farming Games Help.

docs.title: 🤿 Coral Island — Diving & Ocean Restoration Guide
docs.text: Diving is Coral Island's signature mechanic — it replaces the traditional mining gameplay loop with underwater ocean cleaning. Unlike Stardew's mines or Mistria's caverns, diving here is about restoration: collecting trash, salvaging artifacts, and progressively unlocking deeper zones as you clean the ocean floor.

docs.title: 🔓 Unlock Conditions
docs.text:
Requirement Details Day Day 3 (after the introductory quests) Quest "The Ocean Needs You" — delivered via mail after Day 2 Tool Diving gear is provided by Ling (dock, south of farm) Cost Free — no upfront investment Prerequisite Must clear 5 trash from the shoreline (quest step)

Tip: Don't rush to Mining on Day 3. Grab your diving gear from Ling and spend Days 3–5 exclusively underwater. The early trash haul funds your seed purchases for Spring faster than any other activity.

docs.title: 🌊 Ocean Floor Structure
docs.text:
The ocean is divided into 5 distinct zones, unlocked by raising your Ocean Rank via cleaning trash.

Zone Ocean Rank Needed Depth Trash Density Notable Loot 🌿 Shoreline (Zone 1) None 0–5m Medium Glass, Scrap, Common Artifacts 🪸 Coral Garden (Zone 2) Bronze B 5–15m High Kelp, Glass, Shells, Scrolls ⚓ Shipwreck Field (Zone 3) Silver B 15–25m Very High Metal Scrap, Machinery Parts, Gold Chests 🏛️ Ancient Ruins (Zone 4) Gold A 25–40m Medium Precious Relics, Crystals, Rare Artifacts 🌌 Deep Trench (Zone 5) Osmium S 40–60m Sparse Osmium Nodes, Legendary Artifacts

docs.title: Fast Travel Points
docs.text:
Unlocked at Ocean Rank Gold A. Costs 200g to activate each beacon:

Beacon Zone Unlock Cost Dock Beacon Shoreline Free Coral Arch Coral Garden 200g Wreck Bridge Shipwreck Field 400g Temple Gate Ancient Ruins 750g Abyss Entrance Deep Trench 1200g

docs.title: 🗑️ Trash-to-Treasure Conversion
docs.text: Every piece of ocean trash can be processed through recycling machines. This is Coral Island's primary material loop.

docs.title: Complete Recycling Table
docs.text: Trash Type Base Sell Recycled Output Output Sell Value Net Gain 🥤 Plastic Bottle 3g 1 Plastic Scrap 5g +2g 🥫 Tin Can 4g 1 Copper Scrap 8g +4g 🪵 Driftwood 5g 2 Wood 4g −1g 🔩 Rusty Gear 8g 2 Iron Scrap 24g +16g 🧵 Old Cloth 3g 2 Fabric 10g +7g 🪞 Broken Glass 2g 2 Glass 6g +4g 🔗 Rope 2g 1 Fiber 3g +1g ⚙️ Machine Part 15g 1 Gold Scrap 20g +5g ⌛ Ceramic Pot 6g 2 Clay 8g +2g 🔋 Battery 12g 1 Scrap Battery 25g +13g 📦 Cardboard 1g 1 Paper 2g +1g

docs.title: Scrap → Refined Materials
docs.text:
Scrap Type Smelts Into Craft Value Use Copper Scrap ×3 1 Copper Bar 60g Tools, Sprinklers Iron Scrap ×3 1 Iron Bar 150g Upgraded tools, Machines Gold Scrap ×3 1 Gold Bar 400g Late tools, Jewelry Osmium Scrap ×3 1 Osmium Bar 1200g Endgame tools, Auto-collectors Glass ×5 2 Refined Glass 40g Windows, Decorations

Tip: Never sell Glass directly. Store it. Refined Glass is required for the Sprinkler II upgrade and for several museum donation rewards. You'll need 60+ Refined Glass for mid-game automation alone.

docs.title: First Pass (Days 3–15) — Profit Focus
docs.text:
Goal: Clear visible trash in Zone 1 and Zone 2. Sell most recyclable output immediately.

Priority Item Why 1 Rusty Gear Highest net gain per inventory slot (+16g) 2 Battery +13g per item 3 Machine Part +5g per item, but rare 4 Ceramic Pot +2g per item, good volume Everything else — Sell or recycle — don't hoard yet

Expected income: 1500–2500g per diving session (2 in-game hours)

docs.title: Second Pass (Summer+ ) — Materials Focus
docs.text:
Goal: Clean remaining trash, but keep all materials for upgrades.

Keep? Material Reason ✅ Keep Iron Scrap, Gold Scrap Tool upgrades (watering can → iron, axe → gold) ✅ Keep Glass, Refined Glass Sprinkler II × Sprinkler III path ✅ Keep Osmium Scrap Rare — every piece counts for Osmium tools ❌ Sell Plastic, Rope, Cardboard Low value, no important crafts ❌ Sell Driftwood Actually a net loss to recycle (−1g) — sell raw instead

docs.title: 👾 Underwater Enemies
docs.text:
Not all underwater creatures are friendly. These will attack you:

Enemy Zone HP Attack Drop Strategy 🦐 Aggressive Shrimp Zone 1–2 20 Bump damage (8 HP) Shrimp Meat (sell 15g) Just swim away — slower than you 🐡 Pufferfish Zone 2–3 40 Poison spit (15 HP + DoT) Toxic Spine (alchemy) Circle-strafe — spit travels in a straight line 🦑 Ink Squid Zone 3–4 60 Ink cloud (blinds 3s, 12 HP) Ink Sac (sell 25g) Swim upward when cloud appears — cloud sinks 🦈 Reef Shark Zone 4–5 150 Bite charge (40 HP), Tail slap (25 HP) Shark Tooth (sell 80g) Never attack head-on. Hug walls, let it charge past, pick up loot behind it 👻 Phantom Jelly Zone 5 only 80 Shock tether (20 HP + stun 2s) Void Gel (rare crafting) Avoid entirely. Drops nothing worth fighting for

Tip: You don't need to kill anything underwater. Unlike mines, enemies don't block your path — the ocean is open terrain. The only reason to fight is for a specific drop. Sharks drop Shark Tooth (80g) which makes them worth farming in Zone 4 for money.

docs.title: 🏛️ Artifact Collecting
docs.text:
Underwater artifacts are essential for both the museum and cash.

Artifact Zone Rarity Museum Value Sell Price Notes Coral Fragment 1–2 Common 400 rep 50g Extremely common Ancient Coin 2–3 Uncommon 800 rep 200g Also donation quota filler Sea Scroll 3 Uncommon 1000 rep 350g Multiple scrolls needed Gold Idol 3–4 Rare 1500 rep 800g Only 3 in the entire ocean Crystal Skull 4 Rare 2000 rep 1200g Museum centerpiece Star Shard 5 Legendary 3500 rep 2500g Drops from Phantom Jelly (1%) Pearl Necklace 4 Rare 1800 rep 1000g Inside gold chests only Ancient Tablet 5 Legendary 3000 rep 1800g Unlocks secret quest

Museum Donation Priority: 1. Donate 1 of each until the set is complete (bonus reward per set) 2. Sell all duplicates immediately — they're worth more vendor gold than museum black pearls 3. Exception: Star Shard — donate even duplicates; each donation grants +3500 rep (rep accelerates Ocean Rank)

docs.title: 💨 Stamina & Oxygen Management
docs.text:
Diving uses a combined stamina/oxygen resource. Everything costs oxygen.

Action Oxygen Cost Notes Swimming (empty) 1 / sec Base speed Sprint swimming 3 / sec 2× speed, 3× oxygen burn Collect trash 5 per item Open chest 10 per chest Fight (dodge) 15 per dodge Passive regen −1 / 3 sec Only when standing still

docs.title: Oxygen Tank Upgrades
docs.text: Tank Level Max Oxygen Cost Unlock Basic (T1) 200 Free Quest reward Reinforced (T2) 350 500g + 10 Iron Bar Ocean Rank Copper B Deluxe (T3) 550 2000g + 8 Gold Bar Ocean Rank Silver A Premium (T4) 800 5000g + 5 Osmium Bar Ocean Rank Gold A Ultimate (T5) 1200 8000g + 8 Osmium Bar Ocean Rank Osmium S

docs.title: Best Food for Diving
docs.text:
Food Oxygen Restore Effect Season Get Seaweed Salad +50 O₂ None All Craft (Kelp ×2) — costs nothing Grilled Fish +100 O₂ +3 DEF 5 min All Cook (Any Fish) Coconut Water +80 O₂ +1 SPD 3 min Summer Forage from palm trees Clam Chowder +200 O₂ +5 DEF 8 min Spring/Fall Cook (Clam + Milk + Potato) Lobster Bisque +350 O₂ +10 DEF 10 min Fall/Winter Cook (Lobster + Cream + Butter)

Tip: Seaweed Salad is the most oxygen-efficient food in the game. Each Kelp costs 0g (forage from Shoreline) and restores 50 O₂. Carry 10 Seaweed Salads on every dive — they're free, stack, and keep you underwater for an extra 500 O₂ per inventory slot.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis: Diving vs. Fishing vs. Mining
docs.text: Activity Early Game (Spring) Mid Game (Summer) Late Game (Fall+) Best Season 🤿 Diving 1500–2500g/day 2000–3500g/day 3000–5000g/day Spring (no crop income yet) 🎣 Fishing 800–1200g/day 1200–2000g/day 2500–4000g/day Fall (salmon run) ⛏️ Mining 500–1000g/day 3500–6000g/day 5000–8000g/day Summer (gold ore nodes)

docs.title: Seasonal Diving Profit Notes
docs.text: Season Adjustment 🌸 Spring Diving is your #1 income source until crops mature. Day 3–8 diving funds all seed purchases ☀️ Summer Mining overtakes diving. Shift focus — mine for gold, dive only for artifacts and upgrades 🍂 Fall Diving picks up again (Coral Garden has a seasonal artifact spawn event). Shipwreck Field ×2 drops ❄️ Winter Diving is viable year-round. Winter fishing drops but diving stays steady. Use to fund Osmium upgrades

docs.title: 🚀 Deep Dive Tips
docs.text:
1. Inventory management is critical. Each dive yields 20–40 items. If your inventory fills, the best items (gold chests, Star Shards) decay after 30 seconds. Prioritize: Legendary artifacts > Gold chests > Gems > Metal scraps > Glass > Wood.
  2. Zone 2 is your farm spot. Coral Garden has the highest trash density per square meter. Run Zone 2 horizontally, collect everything, surface, recycle, repeat.
  3. Ocean Rank progression path: Bronze B (need 20 trash) → Silver B (need 100 trash) → Gold A (need 300 trash). Each rank doubles the area you can explore. Spend your early game rush on raw trash collection, not artifact hunting.
  4. Upgrade tank before fin speed. More oxygen lets you stay down longer = more trash per session. Fin speed doesn't help if you run out of oxygen.
  5. Gold chests spawn on a timer. Every 5 in-game hours in a fully-cleaned zone, a gold chest respawns. Farm Gold Chest routes once you reach Gold A rank.
  6. Rainy days double trash spawns. The storm stirs up the ocean floor — new trash appears at 2× rate. Dive on every rainy day without exception.

docs.title: Coral Island — Fish Guide & Best Fishing Spots
docs.text:
Complete guide to every fish in Coral Island, including season, location, difficulty, sell price, and usage. Whether you're completing the museum collection or maximizing cooking ingredients, this guide has you covered.

Note: Fish difficulty is rated 1–100 (higher = harder to catch). Some fish are only available at certain times of day or weather conditions.

Level Unlock 1 Basic Rod, Bait unlocked 2 Fiberglass Rod, Small Bait 3 Iridium Rod, Medium Bait 4 Fisher's Net, Large Bait 5 Profession: Fisher (fish sell +25%) or Trapper (crab pots +50%) 6 Quality Bait (attracts silver-quality fish) 7 Deluxe Bait (attracts gold-quality fish) 8 Sonar Bobber (shows fish location while casting) 9 Magnet Bait (catch trash with rare finds) 10 Profession: Angler (fish sell +50%) or Mariner (crab pots no longer catch trash)

docs.title: Arriving on the Island
docs.text: Your first day begins as you step off the boat into the charming town of Coral Island. You'll meet several villagers immediately — follow them to your grandparent's overgrown farm to begin. The tutorial walks you through clearing basic debris, meeting key NPCs, and planting your first seeds.

docs.title: Day 1 Priority List
docs.text:
1. Clear your farmhouse — Place your bed and storage chest inside
  2. Visit Sam's General Store — Buy the cheapest seeds available (parsnips or similar)
  3. Plant and water 6-8 crops — Don't overextend your stamina
  4. Talk to at least 5 villagers — Building relationships early pays off
  5. Check your mailbox — The tutorial quest rewards are inside

docs.title: 💰 Making Money Fast
docs.text:
In the first week, gold is tight. Your best money-makers are:

Method Estimated Daily Income Notes 🌾 Foraging 200-400g Wild fruits, flowers, and shells 🗑️ Trash cleanup 150-300g Sell scrap and recyclable materials 🎣 Fishing 300-600g Requires practice but reliable 🤿 Diving (from Day 3) 500-1000g Best income once unlocked

docs.title: 📅 First Week Schedule
docs.text:
Day Focus Day 1 Plant seeds, meet townsfolk, forage Day 2 Water crops, explore beach area, fish Day 3 Unlock diving — clean ocean trash for big money Day 4 Mine for ore to upgrade tools Day 5 Donate artifacts to museum, unlock new areas Day 6-7 Harvest first crops, reinvest in seeds

Tip: Do not sell raw ore in the first week — you need every scrap for tool upgrades. The pickaxe upgrade to copper should be your first priority, as it dramatically speeds up mining and debris clearing, saving you hours across the first season.

docs.title: ⛏️ Coral Island — Complete Mining Guide
docs.text: Coral Island features three massive mines (Earth, Water, and Wind), each with unique enemies, ores, and rewards. This guide covers floor-by-floor strategy, tool progression, combat, stamina management, and profit optimization.

docs.title: 🔥 Earth Mine (Fire/Lava Themed)
docs.text: The starting mine, unlocked on Day 5 of Spring. 40 floors total, progressively harder.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Boss 1–10 Rocky cavern Copper, Coal Amethyst, Topaz Green Slime, Bat — 11–20 Lava rock Copper, Silver Ruby, Garnet Fire Slime, Lava Bat — 21–30 Magma chamber Silver, Gold Emerald, Sapphire Magma Golem, Fire Spirit — 31–39 Deep magma Gold, Osmium Diamond, Ruby (t2) Lava Titan, Magma Wraith — 40 Boss room — — — Earth Elemental

docs.title: Ore Distribution & Mining Strategy
docs.text:
Floor Range Best For Recommended Approach 1–5 Copper farming, early artifacts Use elevator between 1 and 5; break all rocks 10–15 Silver farming Ignore copper nodes; target dark rocks 20–25 Gold (rare), mid-game artifacts Bring at least Steel Pickaxe 30–35 Gold farming, osmium nodes Bring Gold+ pickaxe and healing items 36–39 Osmium, rare artifacts Save stamina for boss prep 40 Earth Elemental fight Bring 10+ healing items, best weapon

Tip: The elevator unlocks every 5 floors. Always rush to the next elevator floor — don't mine side tunnels until you've unlocked all floors to 40.

docs.title: 🌊 Water Mine (Deep Sea Cavern)
docs.text: Unlocked after Earth Mine completion. 40 floors, water-themed with swimming sections.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Hazards 1–10 Coastal cave Copper, Silver Aquamarine, Pearl Blue Slime, Pufferfish Shallow water 11–20 Underwater tunnel Silver, Gold Sapphire, Coral Electric Eel, Crab Deep water (need breathing) 21–30 Abyssal trench Gold, Osmium Diamond, Moonstone Jellyfish Swarm, Shark Dark water, currents 31–39 Deep ocean floor Osmium, Unknown Metal Black Pearl, Star Gem Kraken Tentacle, Abyss Watcher Low visibility 40 Boss arena — — — Water Elemental

docs.title: Water Mine Specific Mechanics
docs.text:
Mechanic Description How to Handle 🫧 Breathing Underwater sections have oxygen meters Craft Kelp Potion or breathing helmet 🌊 Currents Push you in one direction Walk against current or ride it intentionally 🔦 Visibility Dark sections limit your view range Craft Glow Ring or bring torches 🐚 Shell deposits Unique to water mine, drop pearls Break every one you see — pearls are high value

Unique Loot: Old diving gear, ancient anchors, pearl jewelry, coral fragments.

💡 Tip: Craft at least 5 Kelp Potions before attempting Water Mine floors 20+. Running out of air while surrounded by enemies is a death sentence.

docs.title: 💨 Wind Mine (Sky/Air Themed)
docs.text: Unlocked after Water Mine completion. 40 floors, floating platforms and wind puzzles.

docs.title: Floor-by-Floor Guide
docs.text: Floors Theme Key Ores Gems Enemies Mechanics 1–10 Floating gardens Copper, Silver Citrine, Peridot Sky Slime, Wind Sprite Light gravity 11–20 Skybridge ruins Silver, Gold Topaz, Opal Harpy, Gust Elemental Wind gusts, gaps 21–30 Cloud fortress Gold, Osmium Diamond, Sunstone Storm Golem, Thunder Bird Lightning strikes 31–39 Peak summit Osmium, Stardust Star Gem, Aurora Gem Wind Titan, Sky Leviathan Narrow platforms 40 Storm eye — — — Wind Elemental

docs.title: Wind Mine Specific Mechanics
docs.text:
Mechanic Description How to Handle 🌀 Wind gusts Push you upward or sideways Time your movement between gusts ⚡ Lightning strikes Periodic damage in exposed areas Use lightning rod trinket or time cover ☁️ Cloud platforms Temporary platforms that disappear Move quickly — plan route before stepping 🪶 Feather fall Reduced fall damage in lower gravity Jump further, but control descent

Unique Loot: Feather artifacts, ancient sky maps, storm crystals, air element essence.

⚠️ Warning: Wind Mine has the most complex platforming. If you fall, you lose 5–15 floors of progress. Equip Feather Fall Boots before attempting.

docs.title: ⛏️ Tool Upgrade Progression for Mining
docs.text: Pickaxe Tier Upgrade Cost Required For Efficiency Gain Stamina Reduction 🟢 Bronze 2,000g + 5 Copper Earth Mine 1–15 Base — 🔵 Silver 5,000g + 10 Silver Earth Mine 15–30, Water Mine 1–15 +30% speed -1 stamina/swing 🟡 Gold 10,000g + 8 Gold Earth Mine 30+, Water Mine 15–30 +50% speed -2 stamina/swing 🔴 Osmium 25,000g + 5 Osmium All mines full depth +75% speed -3 stamina/swing 👑 Titanium 50,000g + 10 Titanium Post-game content +100% speed -4 stamina/swing

docs.title: Priority Upgrade Path
docs.text:
1. Bronze → Silver (ASAP) — Unlocks Earth Mine mid-floors and makes early mining bearable
  2. Silver → Gold (before Water Mine) — You'll need Gold to efficiently mine Earth Mine 30+
  3. Gold → Osmium (before Wind Mine) — Osmium is required for top-tier gear
  4. Osmium → Titanium (post-game) — Only for completionists and max efficiency

docs.title: Enemy Reference Table
docs.text: Mine Enemy HP Damage Attack Pattern Dodge Strategy 🔥 Earth Green Slime 30 5 Hop toward you, short range Side step, hit after hop 🔥 Earth Magma Golem 120 25 Slow punch + AOE lava burst Back away, attack after punch 🔥 Earth Lava Titan 250 40 Charge + ground slam Run sideways, not backward 🌊 Water Electric Eel 60 18 Dashes in straight line Sidestep 2 tiles before impact 🌊 Water Kraken Tentacle 200 35 Slam + sweep pattern Jump over slam, duck sweep 🌊 Water Shark 150 30 Rush + bite combo Kite in circles, attack tail 💨 Wind Harpy 45 15 Swoop attack from above Wait for swoop, dodge forward 💨 Wind Storm Golem 180 30 Lightning bolt + wind push Stay behind golem at all times 💨 Wind Sky Leviathan 300 50 Charge beam + tornado Run perpendicular to beam

docs.title: Combat Tips
docs.text:
1. Learn attack patterns — Every enemy telegraphs. Watch for the 0.5s wind-up animation.
  2. Use knockback — Weapons with knockback stats keep enemies away from you.
  3. Food is better than potions — Stack HP recovery meals that heal over time, not instantly.
  4. Bring a ranged weapon — Even a basic slingshot helps against flying enemies (Wind Mine).

docs.title: ⚡ Stamina Management
docs.text: Activity Stamina Cost Efficiency Tip 🪨 Breaking rock (Bronze) 4 stamina Upgrade pickaxe ASAP 🪨 Breaking rock (Osmium) 1 stamina Much cheaper 💎 Breaking gem node 3 stamina Always worth it ⚔️ Weapon swing 2 stamina Don't spam — time attacks 🏃 Sprinting in mine 1 stamina/5 tiles Walk near edges 🪜 Using elevator 0 stamina Free movement between floors

docs.title: Energy Budget for Deep Mining Runs
docs.text: Run Type Target Floors Stamina Budget Food Needed Expected Loot Value 🔰 Casual 1–15 one mine 150 stamina 3 snacks $500–$1,500 ⚡ Efficient Clear floors 10–25 250 stamina 5 meals $2,000–$5,000 🎯 Targeted Specific ore floor 200 stamina 4 meals $3,000–$8,000 🏆 Deep run 30+ in any mine 350+ stamina 8+ high-HP meals $8,000–$20,000 👑 Boss run Floor 40 + boss 400+ stamina 12+ meals + potions $15,000–$30,000

docs.title: 🛡️ Best Gear Setup per Mine
docs.text:
Mine Weapon Ring Boots Clothing Accessory 🔥 Earth Broadsword Fire Resist Ring Normal Boots Leather Armor HP Amulet 🌊 Water Trident Swim Speed Ring Flippers Diving Suit Oxygen Pendant 💨 Wind Dagger (fast) Feather Fall Ring Lightweight Boots Wind Robe Anti-Lightning Charm

Tip: Gear matters more than raw combat stats. A Fire Resist Ring halves lava golem damage in Earth Mine. The right loadout makes each mine 2x easier.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Analysis: Ores vs Gems vs Artifacts vs Geodes
docs.text: Category Avg Value/Hour (Earth Mine) Best For Notes 🪨 Ores (Copper/Silver/Gold/Osmium) $2,000–$5,000 Tool upgrades, crafting Steady, reliable income 💎 Gems (Ruby/Sapphire/Diamond) $3,000–$8,000 Cash, gifts RNG-dependent but high value 🏺 Artifacts $1,000–$12,000 Museum, cash One-time donations worth more 🥚 Geodes (uncracked) $500–$3,000 Unknown loot Gamble — can yield rare items

docs.title: Best Profit Strategy per Mine
docs.text: Mine Primary Profit Source Secondary Avoid 🔥 Earth Mine Gold/Osmium ores + gems Artifacts for museum Low-floor copper after Silver pickaxe 🌊 Water Mine Pearls + star gems Geodes Common shells (low value) 💨 Wind Mine Stardust + rare gems Boss materials Temporary cloud platforms (time waste)

docs.title: Earth Mine
docs.text:
• Secret floor 15b — Hidden behind a cracked wall in the elevator room. Contains a gold vein cluster.
  • Floor 25 shortcut — Jump over the lava stream to reach a hidden chest (always contains a geode).
  • Floor 38 ladder — Hidden behind a dark rock that doesn't sparkle. Break every dark wall on 38.

docs.title: Water Mine
docs.text:
• Floor 10 current — Ride the downward current (don't fight it) to reach a pearl deposit.
  • Floor 22 air pocket — Hidden in the upper-right corner. Restore oxygen here before descending.
  • Floor 35 shortcut — Destroy the cracked coral ring to unlock a direct drop to floor 38.

docs.title: Wind Mine
docs.text:
• Floor 8 cloud bridge — Step on the third cloud from the left → wait 2 seconds → run. Leads to chest.
  • Floor 18 wind tunnel — Face INTO the wind gust and let it push you UP to a hidden platform.
  • Floor 33 lightning rod — Stand under the broken lightning rod during a strike → it powers a teleport pad to floor 36.

💡 Pro Secret: After beating all 3 mine bosses, return to each mine's floor 40. The boss arenas contain hidden chests that respawn every 7 days with rare loot.

Data source: Coral Island v1.0 gameplay data, community testing.

docs.title: 💕 Coral Island — 恋爱社交与婚姻完全指南
docs.text: Coral Island 有 14 位可恋爱角色 + 2 位独特路线，比 Stardew 更丰富的互动系统、多线恋爱机制和分居/离婚选项。本指南帮你规划最优攻略路线。

docs.text: 角色 居住地 职业 生日 日常活动区域 难度 🌊 Rafael 海滩区 潜水教练 春 8 海滩/潜水店 ⭐ 🎸 Charles 起点镇 吉他手/音乐家 春 15 广场/酒吧 ⭐⭐ 🔧 Mark 起点镇 工匠/修理店 夏 10 修理铺 ⭐⭐ 🏥 Kenny 起点镇 医生 夏 20 医院 ⭐⭐⭐ ⚓ Theo 海滩区 渔夫 秋 8 码头/船上 ⭐ 🚜 Scott 起点镇 农机店老板 秋 15 农机店/农场 ⭐ ☕ Ben 起点镇 咖啡馆老板 冬 14 咖啡馆 ⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: 🌸 独特路线（2 位）
docs.text:
角色 解锁条件 特殊机制 备注 🗿 Ling（潜水教练） 解锁潜水后触发 非结婚对象，但有独特好感剧情 好感度满后解锁特殊饰品奖励 👻 Jake 特定剧情选择 非传统恋爱路线 游戏后期解锁

Tip: Alice（渔具店）和 Theo（渔夫）是两性中最容易攻略的角色。他们的最爱礼物都可以钓鱼获得（Alice 喜欢生鱼片，Theo 喜欢稀有鱼），日常也最容易找到。如果想速通结婚成就，选他俩之一的送礼路线。

docs.title: 送礼规则
docs.text: 规则 说明 📊 每周最多送 2 次礼物 超过只有 30% 好感度 🧠 每周最多 1 次最爱礼物 第二个最爱礼物好感度减半 🎂 生日礼物 好感度 ×8，生日必送最爱 💍 送花/香水 恋爱后才解锁，好感度加成 ×2 📈 每周好感度上限 800 经验值/周（约 1~1.5 颗心）

docs.title: 完整送礼偏好速查
docs.text:
角色 🏆 最爱 ❤️ 👍 喜欢 ❌ 厌恶 Alice 🐟 生鱼片、热带鱼 海鲜、贝壳 蘑菇 Surya 🌿 稀有种子、兰花 花卉、盆栽 肉制品 Nina 💃 布料、丝绸 服饰材料 虫饵 Lily 🥧 草莓蛋糕、甜点 水果、糖 鱼（所有） Macy 🛒 咖啡豆、异域香料 杂货类 垃圾 Eva 📚 古籍、神秘卷轴 工艺品 噪音物品 Pablo 🎭 香水、羽毛装饰 奢侈品 廉价物品 Rafael 🌊 潜水装备、珊瑚 海洋产出 人工合成物 Charles 🎸 音乐唱片、手工琴 工艺品 肉类 Mark 🔧 金属锭、工具 矿石、石材 装饰品 Kenny 🏥 药用植物、稀有草药 蔬菜 甜食 Theo ⚓ 稀有鱼类、鱼饵 海鲜 花 Scott 🚜 机械零件、发动机油 矿石、木头 花卉 Ben ☕ 稀有咖啡、茶 饮品 海产

Tip: 最容易批量获取的最爱礼物：Alice 的「热带鱼」（钓鱼获得，夏季多）、Rafael 的「珊瑚」（潜水 Zone1-2 极多）、Theo 的「稀有鱼」（钓鱼浮标机制）。如果多线恋爱，强烈建议用这三位降低礼物成本。

docs.title: 事件触发通用规则
docs.text: 条件 要求 📅 时间段 除深夜（22:00-6:00）外的任何时间 🌤️ 天气 晴天（部分事件需要特定天气） 📍 地点 角色当日所在位置 💕 爱心门槛 每 2~4 颗心触发一个事件 ❌ 不可同时 一次只能触发一个红心事件

docs.title: 关键红心事件选择建议
docs.text: 角色 事件 建议选择 理由 Alice 4❤️ 码头求助 ✅ 帮忙修船 +30 好感，且解锁后期渔业剧情 Alice 8❤️ 表白选择 ✅ 真诚回答 无论选什么都会触发表白，真诚路线最佳 Rafael 6❤️ 潜水事故 ✅ 跳下去救人 好感度大幅提升，触发后续剧情 Lily 6❤️ 烘焙比赛 ✅ 让她自己选配方 尊重选择 → 好感度最高 Scott 4❤️ 农机展览 ✅ 帮忙布置 解锁农机折扣 Theo 8❤️ 船上表白 ✅ 接受邀请 最高好感度增长，错过需再等一周 Surya 6❤️ 植物园 ✅ 选择稀有植物 好感度+50，解锁稀有花种购买 Mark 4❤️ 工坊 ✅ 帮忙修理 —

docs.title: 花束（Bouquet）系统
docs.text: 阶段 物品 获取方式 效果 💐 表白 花束 Pierre's 花店 200g 确认恋爱关系 💍 订婚 戒指 金匠铺 1,000g + 金锭×2 订婚，触发 10❤️ 求婚 🫂 分手 — 自行对话选择 好感度降 5❤️，一周内不可再约

docs.title: 嫉妒机制
docs.text:
情况 效果 惩罚 同时给 2 位候选人送花束 ❌ 触发嫉妒对话 −100 好感度每人 与 A 约会时被 B 看到 ❌ −50 好感度 需送礼物挽回 已婚状态与其他角色有 8❤️+ ❌ 配偶对话警告 −200 好感度 无花束阶段（7❤️前） ✅ 无嫉妒 可安全多线送礼

Tip: 建议 7❤️ 前把所有候选人的礼物拉到同一梯队，选一个最喜欢的送花束确立关系。花束前没有任何惩罚机制，是「广撒网」的安全窗口。花束后每送一朵花给另一个人，之前的爱人都可能吃醋，所以选定了就别乱送。

docs.title: 结婚要求
docs.text: 条件 具体要求 💕 好感度 至少 10❤️（满心） 💐 花束状态 已送花束确认恋爱 💍 订婚戒指 已送出并接受 🏠 房屋升级 至少升级到 Tier 2（有配偶房间） 💰 婚礼费用 婚礼策划 5,000g

docs.title: 婚礼费用明细
docs.text: 项目 费用 说明 💍 订婚戒指 1,000g + 金锭×2 不可省 💐 婚礼策划 5,000g 找社区中心缴费 🏡 房屋升级（Tier 2） 10,000g + 硬木×50 + 金锭×10 必须有配偶单独房间 🎀 可选：婚礼装饰 1,000g 起 不影响功能

docs.title: 室友路线（Roommate Option）
docs.text: 对比项 结婚 室友 好感度门槛 10❤️ 8❤️ 费用 5,000g + 戒指 + 房屋升级 免费 同住 ✅ 搬入农场 ✅ 搬入农场（但分房睡） 配偶日常帮助 ✅ 做饭/浇水/喂动物/修围栏 ❌ 无帮助 星露露早餐 ✅ 配偶每日做早餐 ❌ 孩子 ✅ 可以有 ❌ 不可以 离婚/解除 需找镇长（5,000g） 可选择退出

docs.title: 子女
docs.text: 阶段 触发条件 持续时间 说明 🍼 孕育 结婚 14 天后 14 天 配偶会告知你怀孕的消息 👶 婴儿阶段 14 天孕育期后 14 天 在婴儿床上 👧 幼儿阶段 婴儿期 14 天后 永久 会在农场里奔跑、互动 👦 成长 不进一步成长 — 幼儿形态永久不变

docs.title: 孩子上限
docs.text: 问题 答案 📊 最多几个孩子 2 个（一次最多同时养育） ♂️♀️ 性别 随机，不可选 👶 名字 玩家自定义 🛌 是否需要单独房间 需要 Tier 3 房屋升级（10,000g + 硬木×75 + 金锭×15） 🪄 功能 纯装饰/情感功能，不会帮忙做农活

docs.title: 房屋升级费用总表
docs.text:
升级等级 费用 材料 解锁功能 🏠 Tier 1 默认 无 基础一室一厅 🏠 Tier 2 10,000g 硬木×50 + 金锭×10 配偶房间（结婚必需） 🏠 Tier 3 15,000g 硬木×75 + 金锭×15 + 精炼石英×10 儿童房（有孩子必需） 🏠 Tier 4 20,000g 锇锭×10 + 精炼石英×15 地下室 + 豪华厨房

Tip: 如果想尽快结婚，不要等攒够 10,000g 才升级房屋。先升级工具到铁/金级确保收入的提升，同时给目标角色每天送最爱礼物。通常春季开始送礼，夏季末即可求婚。房屋升级可以放在确立关系（送花束）之后再慢慢攒。

docs.title: ⏱️ 速通结婚路线（最快时间线）
docs.text:
时间点 动作 累计天数 第 1 天 确定目标角色，了解爱好好奇表 D1 第 2~10 天 每天送喜欢礼物，至少 2 次/周 D10 第 14 天 首次达到 2❤️，触发第一个红心事件 D14 第 21 天 每天送礼 + 对话，达到 4❤️ D21 第 30 天 解锁 6❤️ 事件 D30 第 40 天 达到 8❤️，可以送花束确立恋爱 D40 第 42 天 送花束，进入恋爱阶段 D42 第 55 天 达到 10❤️ + 房屋 Tier 2 D55 第 56 天 求婚 → 婚礼！ D56（约 2 季）

最快结婚纪录：约 8 周（游戏内 56 天），一个完整夏季到秋季初。

docs.title: Game Overview
docs.text:
• Developer: Giants Software
  • Platform: PC / PS5 / Xbox Series X|S
  • Genre: Farming Simulation
  • Features: Most realistic machinery simulation

docs.title: Day 1: Taking Over the Farm
docs.text:
1. Sell the starting tractor — too old, inefficient
  2. Rent a medium tractor (daily cost ≈ profit)
  3. Cultivate 3 small fields (about 5-10 acres)
  4. Plant soybeans (highest profit for beginners)

docs.title: Crop Profit Table
docs.text: Crop Growth Period Price/1000L Profit/Acre/Year Soybeans 4 months $3,800 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Corn 6 months $2,200 ⭐⭐⭐ Wheat 5 months $1,800 ⭐⭐ Canola 4 months $3,200 ⭐⭐⭐ Sunflowers 4 months $3,500 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Cotton 8 months $4,500 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Best Investment Path
docs.text: Order Investment Cost Benefit 1 Medium Tractor $150K Cultivation Efficiency +50% 2 Seeder + Harvester $200K Full Mechanization 3 Second Field $100K Double Output 4 Animal Barn $80K Steady Passive Income 5 Biogas Plant $500K Ultimate Passive Income

docs.title: Animal Husbandry Guide
docs.text: Animal Purchase Cost Daily Profit Product Chickens $500 (10 pcs) $200 Eggs Pigs $5,000/head $300 Piglets Cows $3,000/head $500 Milk Sheep $2,000/head $250 Wool

docs.title: Money-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Rent, Don't Buy — Rent equipment instead of buying (save on repairs)
  2. Buy Land When Cheap — June has the lowest land prices
  3. Contract Work — Help NPCs farm to earn money
  4. Sell at Seasonal Peaks — Grain prices fluctuate heavily

docs.title: 🐄 FS25 — Animal Husbandry Deep Dive
docs.text: Data version: FS25 1.5 | Covers all animal types, feed chains, products, and advanced management

docs.title: 🐔 Animal Types & Housing
docs.text:
Animal Purchase Cost Building Building Cost Capacity Chickens $480 (6 chicks) Chicken Coop $18,000 48 Large Coop — Chicken Coop (L) $32,000 96 Dairy Cow $2,500/head Cow Barn $42,000 20 Beef Cow $1,800/head Cow Barn (L) $85,000 50 Sheep $1,200/head Sheep Barn $28,000 30 Pig $1,000/head Pig Sty $35,000 40 Horse $3,500/head Horse Pasture $22,000 6

Tip: Start with chickens — a full coop costs $18,480 and pays for itself in under 2 months.

docs.title: 🌾 Feed Production Chains
docs.text:
Feed Composition Production Method Used By Grass Fresh cut Mow meadow Sheep, Horses, Beef Cows Hay Dried grass Tedder → Windrow → Bale Cows (winter), Sheep Silage Fermented grass/corn Forage harvester → Silo Cows (TMR ingredient) TMR Silage 50% + Hay 30% + Straw 20% Mixer Wagon ($18K) Dairy Cows (max yield) Grain Wheat/Barley/Oats Harvest → Silo Chickens, Pigs, Horses Root Crops Potatoes, Beets Harvest → Root Silo Pigs only

TMR (Total Mixed Ration) boosts dairy cow milk yield by +40% over hay. Prioritize a Mixer Wagon once you have multiple cows.

docs.title: 🥚 Daily Products & Profit
docs.text:
Animal Product Daily Qty Sell Price Revenue/Month ROI Chicken Eggs 0.5–1 L $2,200/1000L $96/mo 0.8 mo Dairy Cow (TMR) Milk 30–55 L $450/1000L $1,500/mo 1.7 mo Dairy Cow (Hay) Milk 20–35 L $450/1000L $936/mo 2.7 mo Sheep Wool 12–20 L $3,800/1000L $672/mo 1.8 mo Horse Riding $150/day rental — $1,800/mo 2.0 mo

Product yield depends on feed quality, cleanliness (−25% if dirty), and health.

Tip: Dairy cows with TMR generate the highest absolute monthly profit. The extra 40% yield easily justifies the Mixer Wagon investment.

docs.title: Transport
docs.text:
Equipment Cost Capacity Animal Trailer (Small) $6,500 8 chickens / 2 cows Animal Trailer (Large) $14,000 16 chickens / 6 cows Loading Ramp $4,200 Required for all

Workflow: Park ramp at pen → back trailer to ramp → press R → load → unload at destination.

docs.title: Manure → Fertilizer Chain
docs.text: Manure Type Source Equipment Field Value Liquid Slurry Pigs Slurry Tanker ($12K–$24K) Saves $150/ha Solid Manure Cows + Pigs (with straw) Manure Spreader ($8K–$35K) Saves $200/ha Compost Solid + Straw Compost Heap ($16K) Saves $300/ha

docs.title: 🤖 Automatic Feeding Systems
docs.text:
System Cost Labor Saved Payback Period Auto-Feeder (Chicken) $6,000 100% feeding 2.2 years Auto-Feeder (Cow) $12,000 100% feeding 4.4 years Auto-Feeder (Sheep) $5,000 100% feeding 1.8 years Robotic Milker $85,000 95% milking 8 years Manure Removal Bot $22,000 80% cleaning 3.5 years

Tip: The Robotic Milker enables a 50+ cow dairy run solo. Without it, large dairies are unmanageable. Buy auto-feeders before the milker.

docs.title: 🌿 Pasture Rotation & Winter Prep
docs.text:
Grass regrowth stages: Short (1d, 1,000L) → Medium (3d, 2,500L) → Long (5d, 4,000L) → Flowering (7d, 5,500L max).

4-paddock rotation: Graze 1 paddock for 3 days, rotate. By day 12, paddock 1 regrows to Stage 3+. Mow excess in summer for winter hay/silage.

Winter: Grass stops Nov–Feb. A 50-cow herd needs 720,000 L of stored feed for 4 winter months. Stockpile hay bales in summer.

docs.title: Quick Tips
docs.text:
1. Chickens first — fastest ROI, learn feed mechanics cheaply
  2. TMR for dairy — 40% yield boost over hay alone
  3. Pigs are fertilizer makers — their value is in the manure cycle, not direct sales
  4. Auto-feeder before milker — feeding is the bigger time sink
  5. Clean barns = healthy animals — dirty barns lose 25% production immediately
  6. Sell wool at seasonal peaks — prices fluctuate like grain

docs.title: 🌽 Farming Simulator 25 Complete Crop Profit Table
Basic Crop Comparison
docs.text: Crop Growth (months) Base Yield Base Price Max Price Profit/Acre Suitable For 🌾 Wheat 8-9 months 2,200L $260 $390 ⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Beginner favorite 🌽 Corn 9-10 months 2,800L $180 $280 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Mid-game 🌻 Sunflowers 7-8 months 1,800L $340 $510 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Steady returns Barley 7-8 months 2,100L $210 $320 ⭐⭐ ❌ Low profit Canola 8-9 months 1,600L $380 $560 ⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ High price Soybeans 9-10 months 1,500L $450 $680 ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ ✅ Profit king Oats 7-8 months 2,000L $230 $350 ⭐⭐ ⚠️ Feed for horses

docs.title: High-Value Crops
docs.text: Crop Features Profit/Acre Best For 🥔 Potatoes High yield, but needs special planter ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mid-game players 🧅 Onions Same as potatoes, needs special equipment ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Mid-game players 🌾 Rice Needs paddy fields, special planting method ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Late-game players 🍇 Grapes Perennial, plant once harvest for years ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Long-term planning 🫒 Olives Same as grapes, needs press equipment ⭐⭐⭐⭐ Long-term planning

docs.title: Woody Crops (Perennial)
docs.text: Crop Planting Cost Annual Maintenance Annual Income Payback Period Overall Rating 🌲 Pine Trees Low Very Low $3,000/acre 4-5 years Plant and forget 🍎 Apples Medium Medium $8,000/acre 2-3 years Good orchard returns 🍇 Grapes Medium-High Medium $12,000/acre 2 years Winery profit

docs.title: Fertilizer Effects
docs.text:
Fertilizations Yield Bonus Suggestion 0 times (no fertilizer) Base yield ❌ Wasting the field 1 time +15% ✅ At least 1 to start 2 times +30% ✅ Must-do at mid-game 3 times (perfect) +50% ⭐ Pursuing maximum yield

Core strategy: Save money with wheat as a beginner → pivot to sunflowers/canola mid-game → plant soybeans + grapes late-game. 2 fertilizations offer the best value; the marginal benefit of a 3rd is diminishing.

docs.title: 🌱 Detailed Crop Data (Per Hectare)
docs.text:
Crop Growth Days Plant Window Harvest Window Price/1000L Avg Yield/ha Revenue/ha Seed Cost Profit/ha 🌾 Wheat 6 Mar–Apr Jul–Aug $260 2,200 L $572 $150 $422 🌽 Corn 9 Apr–May Sep–Oct $180 2,800 L $504 $350 $154 🌻 Sunflowers 7 Apr–May Aug–Sep $340 1,800 L $612 $220 $392 Barley 6 Mar–Apr Jun–Jul $210 2,100 L $441 $140 $301 Canola 7 Mar–Apr Jul–Aug $380 1,600 L $608 $240 $368 Soybeans 8 Apr–May Aug–Sep $450 1,500 L $675 $300 $375 Oats 5 Mar–Apr Jun–Jul $230 2,000 L $460 $130 $330 Sorghum 6 Apr–May Jul–Aug $240 2,300 L $552 $170 $382 🥔 Potatoes 8 Mar–Apr Aug–Sep $480 4,200 L $2,016 $800 $1,216 🧅 Onions 8 Mar–Apr Aug–Sep $520 3,800 L $1,976 $800 $1,176 🌾 Rice 10 Apr–May Sep–Oct $380 3,600 L $1,368 $500 $868 Sugar Beet 9 Mar–Apr Sep–Oct $210 8,000 L $1,680 $900 $780 Cotton 10 Apr–May Sep–Oct $550 2,600 L $1,430 $600 $830

💡 Note: See the Profit Guide for full cost breakdowns including fertilizer, lime, and herbicide costs for each crop.

docs.title: Spring Crops (Plant Mar–Apr)
docs.text: Crop Why Plant in Spring Best For 🌾 Wheat Fast growth, harvest before fall busy season ✅ Beginners, cow feed Barley Quickest turnaround, harvest by Jun ✅ Second crop in rotation Oats Fastest growth (5 days), horse feed ⚠️ Moderate profit Canola High price/1000L, short growth window ✅ Mid-game cash crop 🥔 Potatoes Highest profit per ha, needs full season ✅ Dedicated potato fields Sugar Beet High volume, excellent for processing ✅ Sugar mill chain

docs.title: Summer Crops (Plant Apr–May)
docs.text: Crop Why Plant in Summer Best For 🌽 Corn Thrives in warm weather, highest yield volume ✅ Silage & grain 🌻 Sunflowers Fast summer growth, decent return ✅ Steady income Soybeans Highest base price, profit king ✅ Best ROI per day Sorghum Drought-tolerant, low input cost ✅ Dry or low-fertility fields 🌾 Rice Needs warm water and paddy fields only ✅ Late-game expansion Cotton Long growth but excellent fabric profit ✅ Processing chain synergy

docs.title: Fall Activities (Sep–Nov)
docs.text: Task Details 🚜 Harvest everything Before first frost — postponed harvest loses yield 🌾 Plant Winter Wheat/Barley Grows through winter, harvests early spring next year 🧑‍🌾 Apply fertilizer Prep fields for next season 🛠️ Service equipment Repair & store before winter downtime 📦 Sell grain Check prices — fall often has the year's best prices for grain crops

docs.title: Winter Tasks (Dec–Feb)
docs.text: Task Details 🐄 Animal care Focus on feeding, breeding, and product collection 🪵 Logging / forestry Best off-season income source 🏗️ Build processing plants Flour mill, oil press, bakery, sugar mill 📊 Plan next season Review market trends, pre-order seeds, buy land 🚜 Equipment upgrades Shop off-season sales and used vehicle market

docs.title: 🔄 Crop Rotation Recommendations
docs.text: Plan Field Setup Why It Works 🟢 Beginner 2-Field A: Wheat → Barley; B: Canola → Oats Simple, minimal equipment, steady $ 🟡 Mid-Game 3-Field 1: Potatoes → Winter Wheat; 2: Corn → Soybeans; 3: Sunflowers → Canola Balances high-profit crops with soil-resting periods 🔴 Advanced 4-Field 1: Sugar Beet → Wheat; 2: Potatoes → Barley; 3: Corn → Soybeans; 4: Cotton → Canola Maximum diversity, year-round harvest, processing synergy 🌱 Hay Rotation Grass (3 yr) → Corn (2 yr) → Wheat (1 yr) Low management, perfect for animal feed operations

docs.title: Rotation Tips
docs.text:
• Follow high-profit crops (potatoes/sugar beet) with wheat/barley — they benefit from residual fertilizer
  • Don't plant the same crop twice in a row — yield penalty applies
  • Plant corn after potatoes — potatoes break up soil, corn loves the loose ground
  • Use winter crops to keep fields producing through the cold months

docs.title: Choosing a Map
docs.text:
Map Features Recommendation Riverbend Springs Beginner-friendly, orderly fields ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Hutan Pantai Southeast Asian style, small terraced fields ⭐⭐⭐ Zielonka European style, medium farms ⭐⭐⭐⭐

Recommended for beginners: Riverbend Springs — orderly fields, flat roads, and shops at a reasonable distance.

docs.title: Game Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Recommendation New Farmer Comes with basic equipment and fields, ready to go Best for beginners Farm Manager Plenty of funds, large fields, suitable for experienced players ❌ Don't pick as a beginner Start from Scratch Only empty land + small funds, buy everything yourself ❌ Try after some experience

docs.title: 1️⃣ Get Familiar with Controls
docs.text: Action Key (PC) Get in/out of vehicle F Start engine E Accelerate/Brake W/S Open menu Esc Map M Hire worker H Switch vehicle via Tab Tab

docs.title: 2️⃣ Check Your Assets
docs.text: Press M to open the map and check: - Where your fields are (green highlight) - Where your farm is (house icon) - Where the shop is (tool icon) - Where selling points are (different colored icons)

docs.title: 3️⃣ First Income
docs.text:
Fastest way to make money:

  1. Drive the tractor to your field
  2. Press V to switch tools → select plow or seeder
  3. Press H to hire a worker to help
  4. Drive another vehicle to haul goods for extra cash

docs.title: Crop Selection
docs.text:
Crop Growth Time Profit Good for Beginners 🌾 Wheat Fast Medium ✅ Best choice 🌽 Corn Medium Medium-High ✅ Steady 🌻 Sunflowers Fast Medium ✅ Plantable Barley Fast Low ❌ Low profit Canola Medium Medium ✅ Decent

Suggested first crop: Wheat — grows fast, steady profit, and can feed cows.

docs.title: Planting Process
docs.text:
Plow → Fertilize → Seed → Wait for Growth → Harvest → Sell
 |        |        |          |           |
Optional  Recommended  Required  Watch weather  Watch prices

docs.title: When to Upgrade Equipment?
docs.text: Signal Time to upgrade Always waiting for workers to finish ✅ Buy a second tractor Harvesting too slow ✅ Upgrade harvester No space to store grain ✅ Expand silo Earned $200,000+ ✅ Buy new land

docs.title: Recommended Purchase Order
docs.text:
1. Second tractor + trailer → haul goods for profit
2. Larger seeder → improve efficiency
3. Own harvester → no more renting
4. New land → expand scale
5. Animal pen → raise cows/sheep/chickens

docs.title: Raising Chickens Basics
docs.text: Chickens are the most cost-effective starting livestock: - ❌ Low cost (coop ~$5,000) - ✅ Collect eggs daily, guaranteed profit - ✅ Don't need much feed (free-range works) - ✅ Eggs auto-deliver to the coop, hassle-free

docs.title: ❓ Why did my worker stop moving?
docs.text: Possible issues: tree in the way, out of fuel, tool not lowered. Press H to re-hire.

docs.title: ❓ Not enough money?
docs.text:
• Take out a loan (Press P → Menu → Finances)
  • Do contract missions (blue icons on the map)
  • Start with cheap crops, don't buy the most expensive equipment right away

docs.title: ❓ How to sell grain?
docs.text:
1. Harvested grain is automatically stored in the silo
  2. Drive a trailer to the silo and press R to load
  3. Check which selling point has the best price on the map
  4. Drive there and press R to unload → get paid

docs.title: Efficiency Tips
docs.text:
Tip Description 🏃 Tab to Switch No need to get out, just Tab to switch to nearby vehicles 🗺️ Use Map to Check Prices Real-time prices at each selling point, sell to the highest 🤝 Do Contracts Earn money helping others, and practice with your equipment 💰 Price Fluctuations Wait for high prices to sell, don't rush to sell low 🚜 Rent vs Buy Buy frequently used equipment, rent occasional use

Core strategy: First plant wheat to save your seed money → buy a second vehicle for hauling → expand fields → raise chickens → buy a harvester → scale up planting. By your 3rd hour, you should have a steady cash flow.

docs.title: Tractor Selection
docs.text: Stage Recommended Model HP Price Use 🟢 Starter Fendt 500 Vario 160HP $85,000 Daily farming, sufficient 🟢 Starter Massey Ferguson 8S 180HP $95,000 Slightly pricier but more power 🟡 Mid-Game John Deere 6R 230HP $165,000 One-and-done upgrade 🟡 Mid-Game Fendt 700 Vario 220HP $155,000 Fuel-efficient and reliable 🔴 Late-Game Case IH Steiger 370HP $285,000 Essential for large implements 🔴 Late-Game Fendt 1000 Vario 400HP $350,000 King of the field

docs.text: Stage Recommended Model Power Price Header Sold Separately 🟢 Starter Don't buy, rent early on — — Rental fee $2,000/use 🟡 Mid-Game New Holland CR 480HP $250,000 $35,000 🔴 Late-Game John Deere X9 600HP $380,000 $55,000

docs.text: Tip How Much Saved Details 🏗️ Rent a Harvester Save $250,000+ Harvesting is only used a few times a year; renting saves far more than buying 🔄 Used Tractors Save $40,000 Used equipment is 30-50% off 📦 Buy Bundles Save 15-20% Tractor + implement bundles have a discount 🗺️ Use Contract Equipment Save $0 Try equipment in contract missions to see if it suits you before buying

Real fuel costs matter for large operations. Here's how popular tractors stack up.

Model HP Fuel Use (L/hr avg) Tank Size (L) Runtime per Tank Annual Fuel Cost* Fendt 500 Vario 160 HP 12 L/hr 120 L ~10 hr $1,440 Massey Ferguson 5S.145 145 HP 10 L/hr 95 L ~9.5 hr $1,200 John Deere 6R 150 150 HP 14 L/hr 110 L ~7.8 hr $1,680 Fendt 700 Vario 220 HP 16 L/hr 180 L ~11.2 hr $1,920 Case IH Magnum 250 250 HP 20 L/hr 200 L ~10 hr $2,400 Fendt 1000 Vario 400 HP 28 L/hr 400 L ~14.2 hr $3,360 Case IH Steiger 370 370 HP 30 L/hr 350 L ~11.6 hr $3,600

docs.title: 🏭 Farming Simulator 25 — Production Chains Guide
docs.text: Processing raw crops into finished goods is how you go from broke farmer to agri-empire. This guide breaks down every production chain, its profit margin per step, factory placement strategy, and advanced optimization techniques.

docs.title: 📊 Complete Production Chain Profit Table
docs.text:
All figures per 1,000 L of input. Prices are mid-range — actuals fluctuate with market demand.

Chain Step 1 Value Added Step 2 Value Added Total Added 🌾 Wheat → Flour → Bread Wheat ($260) → Flour ($550) +$290 Flour ($550) + Water → Bread ($1,250) +$700 +$990 🌻 Canola → Oil Canola ($610) → Canola Oil ($1,800) +$1,190 — — +$1,190 🌻 Sunflower → Oil Sunflower ($610) → Sunflower Oil ($1,700) +$1,090 — — +$1,090 🧅 Sugar Beet → Sugar Sugar Beet ($210) → Sugar ($1,400) +$1,190 — — +$1,190 🍇 Grapes → Juice → Wine Grapes ($2,200) → Grape Juice ($3,960) +$1,760 Juice ($3,960) + Aging → Wine ($8,800) +$4,840 +$6,600 🧵 Cotton → Fabric → Clothing Cotton ($1,430) → Fabric ($3,640) +$2,210 Fabric ($3,640) → Clothing ($6,500) +$2,860 +$5,070 🥔 Potatoes → Chips Potatoes ($2,020) → Chips ($5,200) +$3,180 — — +$3,180 🥛 Milk → Cheese → Butter Milk ($800) → Cheese ($2,400) +$1,600 Milk ($800) → Butter ($3,200) +$2,400 +$2,400 🌽 Corn → Silage → Biogas Corn ($504) → Silage ($1,100) +$596 Silage → Biogas ($3,000/yr) +$1,900/yr Recurring

💡 Key Insight: The Grapes → Juice → Wine chain has the highest value-add per liter in the game. A single vineyard + winery setup can generate $100K+/month.

docs.title: 💰 Profit Margin per Step: Raw vs Processed
Grain Chain: Wheat → Flour → Bread
docs.text:
Product Sell Price/1,000L Time to Produce Profit/hr (1 plant) Multiplier 🌾 Raw Wheat $260 0 (harvest) — 1.0x 🌾 Flour $550 20 min $870 2.1x 🍞 Bread $1,250 32 min (total) $928 4.8x

Verdict: Selling raw wheat is a 1x mistake. Flour doubles value. Bread nearly quintuples it.

docs.title: Canola & Sunflower → Oil Chain
docs.text:
Product Sell Price/1,000L Time to Produce Profit/hr Multiplier 🌻 Raw Canola $610 — — 1.0x 🛢️ Canola Oil $1,800 15 min $2,380 2.95x

Verdict: Oil presses have the fastest ROI of any processor. A canola field + oil mill pays for itself in 2 seasons.

docs.title: Grapes → Juice → Wine Chain
docs.text:
Product Sell Price/1,000L Time to Produce Profit/hr Multiplier 🍇 Raw Grapes $2,200 — — 1.0x 🧃 Grape Juice $3,960 25 min $2,112 1.8x 🍷 Wine $8,800 45 min + aging $4,400+ 4.0x

⚠️ Note: Wine requires aging time (in-game hours) before it reaches peak quality and price. Plan your wine cellar capacity accordingly.

docs.title: Sugar Beet → Sugar Chain
docs.text:
Product Sell Price/1,000L Time to Produce Profit/hr Multiplier 🧅 Raw Sugar Beet $210 — — 1.0x 🍬 Sugar $1,400 18 min $2,367 6.7x

Verdict: Sugar from sugar beets has the highest multiplier (6.7x) of any single-step chain. If you have a sugar mill, prioritize sugar beets over every other field crop.

docs.title: Production Chain Factory Locations
docs.text: Factory Best Map Proximity Strategy Purchase Cost 🌾 Grain Mill (Flour) Riverbend Springs Near wheat fields and water $85,000 🛢️ Oil Mill Hutan Pantai Center of map, near canola/sunflower $120,000 🍞 Bakery Riverbend Springs Near grain mill (short wheat/flour haul) $95,000 🍷 Winery Zielonka Vineyard-adjacent, hillside position $180,000 🍬 Sugar Mill Riverbend Springs Near sugar beet fields $110,000 🧵 Spinning Mill Hutan Pantai Near cotton fields, flat terrain $130,000 🧀 Dairy Riverbend Springs Central, between pastures and town $75,000 🏭 Biogas Plant Any Near corn/silage fields, edge of map $500,000

docs.title: Best Map for Production Chains: Riverbend Springs 🌟
docs.text:
Riverbend Springs is the undisputed best map for production chains because:

  1. Central location — All factories can be placed within 500m of each other
  2. Flat terrain — Easier trailer logistics, fewer rollovers
  3. Pre-existing fields — Large field sizes (F1–F10) near water
  4. Short road to sell points — Bakery goods go to the restaurant in town
  5. Water access — Grain mill requires water proximity

Runner-up: Hutan Pantai offers larger contiguous fields but longer road distances. Zielonka is best for vineyard-centric strategies.

docs.title: ⏱️ Time Investment: Manual vs AI Worker Processing
docs.text:
Task Manual Time (10ha) AI Worker Time AI Cost Best For 🌾 Harvest wheat 25 min 40 min $1,200 Let AI do it 🚜 Transport to processor 15 min N/A (you drive) — Always manual 🏭 Load processor 5 min N/A — Quick manual 🍷 Collect wine product 2 min N/A — Quick manual 📦 Deliver to sell point 12 min N/A — Always manual 🌽 Silage harvesting 30 min 50 min $2,500 AI for first pass 🧑‍🌾 Fertilizing 20 min 25 min $800 AI is cheaper

Strategy: Use AI workers ONLY for field work (cultivating, seeding, fertilizing, harvesting). Always handle transport, processing, and selling yourself — those are high-value, low-time tasks.

docs.title: 🌦️ Seasonal Considerations
docs.text: Chain Best Season Worst Season Seasonality Strategy 🌾 Grain → Flour → Bread Harvest (Jul–Aug) Winter Process in fall/winter when prices are higher 🌻 Canola/Sunflower → Oil Post-harvest (Aug–Sep) Spring Store oil, sell before next harvest dip 🍇 Grapes → Juice → Wine Harvest (Aug–Sep) Winter Wine improves with aging; sell next summer 🧅 Sugar Beet → Sugar Harvest (Sep–Oct) Spring Sugar doesn't spoil — hold for best price 🧵 Cotton → Fabric Harvest (Sep–Oct) Spring Fabric prices stable year-round 🥛 Dairy processing Year-round None Steady daily income, no seasonality

docs.title: Year-Round Production Strategy
docs.text:
Production Type Input Source Winter Viable? Requires 🏠 Greenhouse vegetables Indoor planting ✅ Yes Heated greenhouse (FS25) 🐄 Dairy barn Hay, silage (stored) ✅ Yes Barn heater + stored feed 🧀 Cheese/Butter Milk from barn ✅ Yes Dairy + barns 🏭 Oil mill Stored canola/sunflower ✅ Yes Adequate silo storage 🍞 Bakery Stored flour ✅ Yes Grain mill + flour storage 🌽 Biogas Corn silage (stored) ✅ Yes Silage bunker + biogas plant

Tip: Build at least 2M L of silo storage before winter. Process throughout the cold months when field work is impossible — this turns idle time into $40K–$80K/month.

docs.title: Quality Bonuses from Precision Farming
docs.text:
With the Precision Farming DLC, fertilizer quality directly impacts processing yields:

Fertilization Level Yield Bonus Processing Output Bonus Total Chain Profit Impact 0x (No fertilizer) Base 100% Base 100% Baseline 1x Fertilized +15% yield +5% processing output +9–15% chain profit 2x Fertilized +30% yield +10% processing output +20–28% chain profit 3x (Perfect) +50% yield +15% processing output +33–42% chain profit

docs.title: Optimal Fertilizer Strategy per Chain
docs.text: Chain Recommended Fertilization Why 🌾 Wheat → Bread 3x perfect High-value end product; every quality point multiplies 🍇 Grapes → Wine 2x (optimal) Grapes are perennial; third application has diminishing returns 🌻 Canola → Oil 2x (cost-effective) Oil mill already doubles value; extra fert is lower priority 🧅 Sugar Beet → Sugar 2x Sugar beet already high yield; 3rd pass marginal 🧵 Cotton → Fabric 3x perfect Fabric price high enough to justify perfect fert 🥔 Potatoes → Chips 2x Chips already 2.5x multiplier; fert at 2x is sweet spot

docs.title: Integrated Farm Strategy
docs.text:
graph TD
    A[Field Crops] --> B[Harvest & Store]
    B --> C{Process or Sell?}
    C -->|Sell raw| D[Immediate cash - low profit]
    C -->|Process| E[Factory]
    E --> F[Store finished goods]
    F --> G{Sell timing?}
    G -->|Off-peak demand| H[Wait for high price]
    G -->|Need cash| I[Sell at current price]
    H --> J[MAX PROFIT]
    I --> K[Lower profit but liquid]
    A --> L[Fertilize 2x min]
    L --> A

Pro Strategy: Never sell raw crops after Year 1. By Year 2, every field should feed into at least one processing chain. The Grapes→Wine chain alone can generate $300K+/year from a single hectare of vineyard.

Data source: Farming Simulator 25 gameplay data, community testing, precision farming DLC mechanics.

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Complete Profit Guide
docs.text: Understanding crop profitability is the foundation of a successful farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide breaks down every major crop by revenue, cost, net profit per hectare, growing time, and seasonal considerations to help you maximize your bottom line.

docs.title: Crop Profitability Table
docs.text:
All figures are per hectare (ha) and assume average yield with standard fertilization (no precision farming bonuses). Prices fluctuate based on market demand — the values below represent typical mid-range prices.

Crop Revenue/ha Seed Cost/ha Fertilizer Cost/ha Net Profit/ha Growth Time Harvest Months Wheat $2,200 $150 $200 $1,850 6 days Jul–Aug Barley $2,100 $140 $200 $1,760 6 days Jun–Jul Oats $2,000 $130 $200 $1,670 5 days Jun–Jul Canola $3,000 $240 $250 $2,510 7 days Jul–Aug Sunflower $2,800 $220 $250 $2,330 7 days Aug–Sep Soybeans $3,800 $300 $250 $3,250 8 days Aug–Sep Corn (Maize) $4,200 $350 $300 $3,550 9 days Sep–Oct Potatoes $6,500 $800 $400 $5,300 8 days Aug–Sep Sugar Beet $7,200 $900 $400 $5,900 9 days Sep–Oct Cotton $5,500 $600 $350 $4,550 10 days Sep–Oct Sorghum $2,400 $170 $200 $2,030 6 days Jul–Aug Rice (Paddy) $4,800 $500 $350 $3,950 10 days Sep–Oct Grapes $9,000 $2,000 $500 $6,500 Perennial Aug–Sep Olives $8,000 $1,800 $500 $5,700 Perennial Sep–Oct Poplar $3,200 $400 N/A $2,800 12 months All year

docs.title: Profit per Day Analysis
docs.text:
When planning your crop rotation, profit per day (net profit ÷ growth time) gives the truest picture of efficiency.

Crop Net Profit/ha Growth Days Profit/Day Sugar Beet $5,900 9 $655 Potatoes $5,300 8 $663 Corn $3,550 9 $394 Rice $3,950 10 $395 Soybeans $3,250 8 $406 Grapes $6,500 Perennial ~$300/day (over 22 months) Canola $2,510 7 $359 Cotton $4,550 10 $455 Sunflower $2,330 7 $333 Wheat $1,850 6 $308 Barley $1,760 6 $293

Top 3 by Profit/Day: Potatoes ($663), Sugar Beet ($655), Cotton ($455).

docs.title: Crop Products & Processing Profit
docs.text: Processing raw crops into higher-value products dramatically increases profitability. Below are the most lucrative processing chains.

docs.title: Grains & Flour
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Flour 100 L Wheat ($55) $110 +$55 Bread 100 L Flour ($110) + 20 L Water $250 +$140 Cake 100 L Flour + 50 L Milk + 30 L Eggs $380 +$230 Oat Flakes 100 L Oats ($50) $120 +$70

docs.title: Sunflower & Canola → Oil
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Canola Oil 100 L Canola ($75) $180 +$105 Sunflower Oil 100 L Sunflowers ($70) $170 +$100

docs.title: Sugar Beet → Sugar
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Sugar 100 L Sugar Beets ($40) $140 +$100 Cake (uses Sugar) 100 L Sugar + other inputs $380 Variable

docs.title: Grapes → Juice → Wine
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Grape Juice 100 L Grapes ($200) $360 +$160 Wine 100 L Grape Juice ($360) + aging $800 +$440

docs.title: Cotton → Fabric
docs.text: Product Raw Input Output Value Value Added Fabric 100 L Cotton ($120) $280 +$160 Clothing 100 L Fabric ($280) $500 +$220

docs.title: Seasonal Considerations
docs.text:
Farming Simulator 25 features a dynamic seasonal cycle that affects crop viability.

Season Best Crops to Plant Notes Spring (Mar–May) Wheat, Barley, Oats, Canola, Potatoes Prepare fields, fertilize before planting Summer (Jun–Aug) Corn, Soybeans, Sunflowers, Sorghum Fast growth, good weather windows Fall (Sep–Nov) Harvest most crops, plant winter wheat/barley Busiest season — plan equipment carefully Winter (Dec–Feb) Animal care, maintenance, logging Minimal field work; focus on animals and processing

docs.title: Rotation Strategy
docs.text:
A 3-field rotation maximizing profit:

  • Field 1: Potatoes (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Winter Wheat (Sep)
  • Field 2: Corn (Spring) → Harvest (Sep) → Fallow/Green Manure
  • Field 3: Soybeans (Spring) → Harvest (Aug) → Canola (Winter)

docs.title: Cost-Saving Tips
docs.text:
1. Fertilize efficiently — use precision farming to apply variable-rate fertilizer only where needed, saving 20–30% on input costs.
  2. Own your equipment — leasing is great for specialized harvesters, but buying tractors and basic tools pays off after 3 seasons.
  3. Sell at peak prices — crop prices fluctuate weekly. Store grain in silos and sell when prices are 15–20% above average.
  4. Use cover crops — planting grass or clover between cash crops reduces fertilizer needs by 50%.
  5. Lime management — test soil pH and apply lime only when required. Over-liming wastes money without additional yield benefit.
  6. Weed control — mechanical weeding (hoeing) costs less than herbicide and doesn't degrade soil quality.

docs.title: Animal Profitability
docs.text:
If you diversify into animal husbandry, here are the profit estimates per animal per year:

Animal Initial Cost Annual Profit Products Chicken $150 each $1,200/yr Eggs, Meat Sheep $500 each $2,500/yr Wool, Meat Cow (Dairy) $1,200 each $4,000/yr Milk, Meat Pig $400 each $3,000/yr Meat, Manure Horse $3,000 each $500/yr Breeding (harder to profit)

Best Animal Investment: Dairy cows offer the highest annual return, but require the most investment in barns and feeding equipment. Chickens are the easiest entry point with the lowest startup cost.

docs.title: 📊 Crop vs Animal ROI Comparison
docs.text:
Which earns more — planting crops or raising animals? Here's the per-hectare equivalent comparison.

Enterprise Startup Cost (per unit) Annual Profit (per unit) Land Needed Labor Risk Level ROI Year 1 🌾 Wheat (1 ha) $350 (seed+fert) $1,850 1 ha Low Low 528% 🌻 Sunflowers (1 ha) $470 $2,330 1 ha Low Low 495% 🌽 Corn (1 ha) $650 $3,550 1 ha Medium Medium 546% 🥔 Potatoes (1 ha) $1,200 $5,300 1 ha High Medium 441% 🍇 Grapes (1 ha) $2,500 $6,500 1 ha Medium Low (perennial) 260% 🐔 Chickens (10) $1,500 (coop+birds) $1,200/yr Tiny Very Low Low 80% 🐑 Sheep (5) $5,000 (pasture+sheep) $2,500/yr 0.5 ha Low Low 50% 🐄 Dairy Cows (5) $15,000 (barn+cows) $4,000/yr 1 ha (feed) High Medium 27% 🐖 Pigs (5) $7,000 (pen+pigs) $3,000/yr 0.5 ha Medium Medium 43%

docs.title: Key Insight
docs.text: Crops give faster ROI but require seasonal work. Animals provide steady daily income but need higher upfront investment. Start with crops, add chickens for daily cash flow, then scale to cows as you build feed production.

docs.title: 💼 Contract Work Income Table
docs.text:
Contracts are a great early-game money source. Here's what each type pays.

Contract Type Avg Payout Time Needed Hourly Rate Equipment Provided? Verdict 🌾 Harvest (small field) $3,000–$5,000 10–15 min $12,000–$20,000/hr ✅ Yes (harvester + trailer) ⭐⭐⭐ Best starter 🌾 Harvest (medium field) $8,000–$15,000 20–40 min $12,000–$22,500/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🌾 Harvest (large field) $20,000–$40,000 60–90 min $15,000–$26,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🚜 Cultivate $2,000–$8,000 15–30 min $8,000–$16,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐ 🧑‍🌾 Sowing/Planting $2,500–$10,000 15–40 min $6,000–$15,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐ 🪵 Logging $5,000–$20,000 30–60 min $5,000–$20,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐ (requires skill) 🧹 Baling $3,000–$12,000 20–45 min $6,000–$16,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐ 🥔 Potato Harvest $10,000–$25,000 30–60 min $10,000–$25,000/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐⭐ 🐄 Transport Animals $1,500–$4,000 5–10 min $12,000–$24,000/hr ✅ Trailer ⭐⭐ (one-off) 🌾 Weeding/Spraying $2,000–$6,000 10–25 min $8,000–$14,400/hr ✅ Yes ⭐⭐⭐

docs.title: Contract Strategy
docs.text:
Phase Strategy Expected Income 🔰 Day 1–5 Do all harvest contracts — highest pay, equipment included $15K–$30K/day 🟢 Week 2–3 Keep doing harvest + cultivate; buy your own tractor + trailer $30K–$60K/day 🟡 Month 2+ Be selective — only take contracts near your fields $50K–$100K/day 🔴 Late game Skip contracts — your own farm earns more per hour N/A

💡 Pro tip: Accept a contract, then use the leased equipment for your own fields too! The rental fee is already covered by the contract.

docs.title: 🏆 Best Investment Order (Expanded)
docs.text: Priority Investment Cost Monthly Return Payback Period Alternative 1 🚜 Medium Tractor (150 HP) $80K +50% field speed Immediate Rent first if low cash 2 🌾 Seeder + Planter $65K +100% planting speed 2 seasons Rent for first season 3 🗺️ Buy 2nd field (small) $100K $3K–$5K/month 8–12 months Lease field if cheaper 4 🚛 Larger trailer (26K L) $55K Fewer trips = more $ 3–4 seasons Skip if solo farming 5 🐔 Chicken coop (10 hens) $1.5K $100/month 15 months Best early passive income 6 🌾 Combine harvester $250K Save $2K/rental × ~20 uses 6–8 years rent savings Keep renting until 5+ fields 7 🧑‍🌾 Fertilizer spreader + sprayer $60K +30% yield on all fields 2–3 seasons Rent first 8 🐄 Dairy barn + 5 cows $20K $1.5K/month 13 months Start with chickens first 9 🏗️ Grain dryer (corn/rice) $75K Premium prices for dried grain 3–4 seasons Only needed for certain crops 10 🏭 Oil mill / Flour mill $150K Double crop value 5–8 seasons Game-changer when processing 11 🗺️ Buy larger fields $200K+ Scales all profit 3–6 months Always a good investment 12 🏗️ Biogas plant $500K $5K–$10K/month 4–8 years Only after 50+ cows or 100+ acres 13 🏪 Farm shop / Direct sales $100K +30% sell price 4–6 seasons Best late-game upgrade

docs.title: Early Game Priority (First $500K)
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Start] --> B[Medium Tractor]
    B --> C[Seed & Fertilize]
    C --> D[Buy 2nd Field]
    D --> E[Harvester - Rent]
    E --> F[Chickens for passive $]
    F --> G[Grain Dryer or Oil Mill]
    G --> H[Expand to 5+ Fields]

docs.title: Mid-Game Priority ($500K–$1.5M)
docs.text:
1. Own a combine harvester — stop renting, keep full harvest profit
  2. Build processing chain — oil press for canola/sunflower, flour mill for wheat
  3. Add dairy cows (5–10 head) — steady milk income
  4. Buy precision farming gear — variable-rate fertilizer saves 20–30%
  5. Expand to 10+ fields — scale is the ultimate profit multiplier

docs.title: Late Game ($1.5M+)
docs.text:
1. Biogas plant — turns silage into $10K+/month passive income
  2. Farm shop — sell processed goods at retail prices
  3. Greenhouses — year-round vegetable production, minimal labor
  4. Buy every field on the map — total farmland dominance

Data source: Farming Simulator Wiki 1.0

docs.title: Farming Simulator 25 — Vehicle Comparison Guide
docs.text: Choosing the right tractor, harvester, or specialized vehicle is critical to running an efficient farm in Farming Simulator 25. This guide compares key specifications including horsepower, top speed, price, and fuel capacity to help you make informed purchasing decisions.
